title: Conductive nano nickel oxide/hydroxide paper electrochemical sensor for serotonin detection in genetically engineered Drosophila
abstract: A sensor for the detection of serotonin concentration in genetically engineered Drosophila by using an electrochemically active conductive nano nickel oxide/hydroxide composites.

title: Correction: Galactosylated silver nanoparticles as a biocompatible intrinsic SERS probe for bladder cancer imaging and ex vivo tumor detection
abstract: Correction for ‘Galactosylated silver nanoparticles as a biocompatible intrinsic SERS probe for bladder cancer imaging and ex vivo tumor detection’ by Ting-Yu Cheng et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TB00546A.

title: Overcoming cancer immunotherapy barriers via nanomaterial-mediated pyroptosis
abstract: While cancer immunotherapy has achieved groundbreaking clinical success, its efficacy is frequently compromised by insufficient T-cell activation, the immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment (TME), and off-target toxicity.

title: Metallic nanomaterials in Parkinson's disease: a transformative approach for early detection and targeted therapy
abstract: This review article illustrates and discusses the application of metallic nanomaterials (MNMs) in the detection and treatment of PD, as well as theranostic MNMs.

abstract: GOD@ZIF-7-III composites demonstrate 98% catalytic activity, improved pH and thermal stability, 66% storage retention, and efficient glucose detection in plasma, highlighting biosensing potential.

abstract: Dense biological tissues present formidable transport barriers that limit therapeutic penetration. High intensity focused ultrasound was applied to acoustically-responsive nanoparticles to propel them through tissue mimics composed of gelatin.

title: A PEGylated conjugated-BODIPY oligomer for NIR-II imaging-guided photothermal therapy
abstract: We have developed self-assembled nanoparticles (BODIPY 4 -PEG NPs) that exhibit excellent imaging performance and photothermal conversion efficiency.

title: Light-activated spatiotemporal control over nanoreactor permeability
authors: Rolf A. T. M. van Benthem
abstract: Bicontinuous nanospheres can be transiently switched using light between an open active state and a closed inactive state. This behavior is retained when the particles are embedded in a hydrogel.

title: Engineered decalcified bone matrix/barium titanate piezoelectric composite scaffolds for bone regeneration
abstract: A schematic diagram illustrating the fabrication process and biological application of a three-dimensional composite scaffold containing piezoelectric stimulation and synergistically engineered DBM for osteoinductive factors.

abstract: Continuous real-time monitoring of non-cognitive markers is essential for the preliminary detection, control, and regulation of long-standing health conditions.

title: Reversible photo-induced formation of iron alginate hydrogels
abstract: This work represents reversible formation and dissolution of iron alginate hydrogel, which can be induced by different wavelength visible light.

title: Recent advances in NIR-II emitting nanomaterials: design and biomedical applications of lanthanide complexes and functionalized mesoporous silica nanoparticles (MSNs)
abstract: This work highlight advances in NIR-II nanomaterials, focusing on Ln-doped systems and Ln-MOFs via various synthesis methods. Functionalized MSNs show promise for imaging, therapy, and delivery, offering tunable, biocompatible theranostic potential.

abstract: The HGP@EGCG hydrogel with suitable adhesion, low swelling, and good moisture retention is developed by self-assembly and physical and chemical crosslinking. It can eradicate bacteria, promote angiogenesis, and accelerate wound healing in rats.

title: MXenes and MXene-based composites for biomedical applications
abstract: MXenes, a novel class of two-dimensional materials, have recently emerged as promising candidates for biomedical applications due to their specific structural features and exceptional physicochemical and biological properties.

title: Addressing the polycation dilemma in drug delivery: charge-converting liposomes
abstract: The developed liposomes offer a novel strategy to overcome the polycation dilemma by improving mucus permeation and cellular uptake. Enzymatic activation triggers a surface charge shift to positive, enhancing interaction with epithelial cells.

title: CVD-grown MXene–CNT nanocomposite-assisted electrochemical immunosensors for label-free and ultra-sensitive monitoring of thyroid-stimulating hormone in artificial serum
abstract: A CVD-grown 3D MXene–CNT nanocomposite enables a robust, antifouling electrochemical interface for covalent antibody binding and label-free, ultra-sensitive TSH detection in artificial serum.

title: Near infrared-triggered diketopyrrolopyrrole nanoparticles for photothermal applications against bacterial infections
abstract: Wound infection caused by bacteria is an important factor that affects the rapid healing of wounds, and the emergence of drug-resistant strains complicates the treatment, which poses great challenges to surgeons.

title: An intelligent ZIF-based nanoplatform for photothermal/chemodynamic-induced combination therapy with O 2 evolution properties for improved infected wound regeneration
abstract: A multifunctional ZIF-based nanosystem incorporating Cu ions and a phytosterol showed excellent antibacterial and photothermal activities, making it a promising wound healing candidate.

abstract: We developed ICP@PDA-PP@HA NPs for improved CPT-11 delivery. NPs induce immunogenic cell death via multiple pathways, which, combined with PTT and anti-PD-L1, results in 95.8% tumor inhibition and 100% eradication without recurrence.

abstract: We present a novel glycosylated polyplex micelle system derived from charged glycosylated block copolymers. This approach offers a promising pathway toward the design of more intricate glycosylated nanoparticles.

title: Bioorthogonal reaction mediated size transformation clustered nanosystems for potentiating bioimaging and drug delivery
abstract: This review highlights bioorthogonal reaction mediated size transformation clustered nanosystems, summarizing the bioorthogonal reaction-based design principles and the effectiveness on bioimaging quality and therapeutic outcomes.

title: Quercetin-doped sol–gel coatings on titanium implants: a promising approach for enhanced immune response and cell adhesion
abstract: This study aimed to enhance cell response to titanium surfaces doped with quercetin coatings, correlating in vitro findings with protein adsorption patterns.

title: Bimetallic doped carbon dot nanozymes for enhanced sonodynamic and nanocatalytic therapy
abstract: Bimetallic doped carbon dots (CDs) were prepared as a high-efficiency nanozyme and sonosensitizer for enhanced SDT and NCT.

title: Cellulose-based bioactive material and turmeric-impregnated flexible and biocompatible scaffold for bone tissue engineering applications
abstract: Cellulose-based cotton fabric impregnated with a bioactive material and turmeric acts as a flexible, biocompatible, eco-friendly, and cost-effective material for bone regeneration applications.

title: Precision-engineered supramolecular assembly of bio-MOFs to combat antimicrobial resistance
abstract: Bio-MOF releasing metal ions (M n + ), acting as molecular scissors in eradicating viral, bacterial and fungal infections.

title: A multifunctional piezoelectric ZIF-8/PVDF fiber dressing for managing infected wound healing
abstract: The multifunctional piezoelectric ZIF-8/PVDF fiber dressing generates electrical stimulation under ultrasound, which synergizes with its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and angiogenic properties to significantly accelerate infected wound healing.

abstract: Poly(ethylene glycol) (PEG)-based hydrogels are brittle and bioinert. Incorporating a bioactive self-assembling tryptophan zipper (trpzip) peptide during polymerization enhanced bioactivity, durability and self-healing properties.

abstract: Extrudable hyaluronic acid hydrogel inks modified with metal organic frameworks exhibit anti-swelling and pH-dependent sustained release profiles of polyphenol drugs for wound-healing applications, and enable 3D printing with high fidelity.

title: Intercalating cerium into layered double hydroxide as a promising reactive oxygen species scavenger for inflammation treatment
abstract: We report the development of cerium doped layered double hydroxide nanoparticles for eliminating reaction oxygen species production during inflammation.

title: miRNA electrochemical biosensors based on MXene and MBene nanomaterials: recent advances and future perspectives
abstract: MXenes and MBenes are 2D nanomaterials with promising capabilities in miRNA detection. They demonstrate considerable promise in designing miRNA biosensors, opening up new avenues for further research and development.

title: Smart biosensors with self-healing materials
abstract: This review presents the latest innovations in smart and self-healing biosensors, covering a range of designs for different biomedical applications.

Mater. Chem. B , 2025, 13 , 9392–9406, https://do…

abstract: The α-hydroxymethyl-α,β-unsaturated carbonyl (HMUC) scaffold represents a valuable framework for constructing nucleophile-responsive materials.

abstract: An illustration of 2TB-NPs@TM nanocarrier and photothermal therapy targeting ovarian cancer cells. A biomimetic nanocarrier integrating homologous targeting, NIR-II imaging, and photothermal therapy for precise ovarian cancer treatment.

abstract: A RuN 4 single-atom nanozyme was synthesized and it exhibited multi-enzymatic activities, which effectively alleviated eczema symptoms by ROS clearance.

abstract: NiCo 2 O 4 –Ti 3 C 2 T x nanohybrid based electrochemical sensor was prudently designed, which afforded abundant electrocatalytic sites, enhanced conductivity and impressive analytical performances at applied potentials of +0.5 and −0.25 V for glucose and H 2 O 2 .

abstract: An extracellular matrix mimicking bilayer wound dressing, combining a hydrogel and electrospun nanofibers, offers multifactorial pro-healing features for enhanced skin wound healing.

abstract: In silico screening and cell line-based validation of quercetin to target lung fibrosis. Preparation, optimization and characterization of a chitosome formulation with sustained release for prospective targeted drug delivery.

abstract: Nanocomposites co-delivering TRAIL plasmid and salinomycin potentiate TRAIL-induced apoptosis in carcinoma through coordinated modulation of the Wnt/β-catenin, STAT3, and c-Myc signaling axes coupled with ROS upregulation.

abstract: The use of carbonised porous silicon (C-pSi) substrates as both scaffolds and electrodes for the electrochemical detection and characterisation of bacterial biofilm growth is reported for the first time.

title: Wearable microfluidic immunosensors for point-of-care detection of bio-analytes: a critical review
abstract: In the futuristic era of mHealth and personalized medicine, a game-changing solution for convenient, timely, and point-of-care diagnosis is the integration of miniaturized microfluidics devices and antibody-based biosensors into wearable designs.

title: Surface modification of polycaprolactone with sulfated alginate for enhanced binding of a heparin-binding protein
abstract: Optimising surface modification of PCL and evaluating solution stability of surface layer. Evaluation of lactoferrin interaction with solution and surface bound polysaccharide.

title: Chromogenic ecdysis of Au@Ag nanorods assembled in a hydrogel for selective detection of ammonia vapors in food
abstract: A chromogenic hydrogel sensor containing Au-Bead@Ag nanorods incorporated inside agarose gel for visual detection of ammonia released from spoiled food.

title: Development of cysteine-sensitive bimodal probes for in situ monitoring of early-stage pulmonary fibrosis progression and therapeutic effects
abstract: A novel Cys probe MR-Cys for monitoring the pulmonary fibrosis progression and therapeutic effects.

abstract: The increasing prevalence of antimicrobial resistance and adverse effects of systemic treatments calls for urgent reevaluation of current methods that rely on excessive, uncontrolled drug administration.

abstract: We developed a buoyant plasmonic Au/microbubble (MB) substrate functionalized with copper ions (Cu 2+ ) and 4-mercaptobenzoic acid (4-MBA) to establish a SERS-based sensing platform for detecting amyloid-β (Aβ) proteins.

abstract: This review elucidates cultivation-phase intervention strategies that actively enhance cell sheet functionality through endogenous bioactive modulation and exogenous scaffold co-culture, driving functional enhancement and regenerative breakthroughs.

abstract: This review explores stimuli-responsive biomaterials for melanoma treatment, addressing drug delivery and tumor microenvironment challenges with pH, light, temperature, ROS, and enzyme-responsive systems.

title: Tissue-adhesive, antibacterial, and macrophage-reprogramming hydrogel for sealing colorectal anastomotic leakage and promoting healing
abstract: A multifunctional hydrogel with robust mechanical sealing and antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and tissue-healing properties improves anastomotic healing. This multi-faceted strategy is both scientifically innovative and clinically relevant.

abstract: pH-responsive sodium alginate/polyvinylpyrrolidone/Zr–metal organic framework composite for controlled lapatinib release and high biocompatibility in oral breast cancer therapy.

title: Engineering of redox-triggered polymeric lipid hybrid nanocarriers for selective drug delivery to cancer cells
abstract: Synthesis and fabrication of redox-switchable polymeric lipid hybrid nanocarriers that selectively deliver berberine and induce apoptosis in GSH-rich cancer cells while sparing healthy cells.

abstract: Fine-tuning polymeric membrane composition of pH responsive and photo crosslinked polymersomes containing ferrocence moieties into the hydrophobic membrane.

Mater. Chem. B , 2017, 5 , 8133–8142, https://doi.org/10.1039/C7TB01890H.

abstract: Silk fibroin scaffolds loaded with adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells reverse liver fibrosis by reducing inflammation and enhancing fatty acid metabolism pathways.

abstract: This manuscript innovatively takes macrophages as the carrier and cholesterol metabolism as the core, describing in detail the topic of immune escape. It also reviews nanomaterials and clinical strategies for targeting cholesterol metabolism.

title: Recent advances in natural product-based drug delivery systems for the treatment of osteoarthritis
abstract: This review summarizes natural products’ mechanisms and latest delivery advances.

abstract: Carbon-dot-sensitized hollow Co 9 S 8− x was synthesized as inorganic sonosensitizer for enhanced sonodynamic tumor therapy. The presence of sulfur vacancies and the fabrication of heterojunctions realize the cascaded amplification of ROS production.

title: Correction: An implantable composite scaffold for amplified chemodynamic therapy and tissue regeneration
abstract: Correction for ‘An implantable composite scaffold for amplified chemodynamic therapy and tissue regeneration’ by Jiafei Chen et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2023, 11 , 3151–3163, https://doi.org/10.1039/D2TB02699F.

abstract: Capping of lanthanide-based nanoparticles with Gum Arabic improved their dispersibility in biologically relevant media, chemical as well as optical stability, and biocompatibility towards cells and zebrafish.

abstract: To address the problems associated with pathogenic bacteria in healthcare settings, the development of novel antibacterial materials is of high priority.

abstract: Thermoresponsive CDs–agar–PNM nanocomposites integrate photothermal carbon dots and PNIPAM–agarose shells, exhibiting tunable LCST behavior, efficient light heating, and temperature-controlled curcumin release for biomedical applications.

abstract: Vegetable oil-derived sustainable alternatives to petrochemical-based synthetic polymers for biomedical applications.

abstract: A novel ECM-mimetic silk fibroin mesh with micro/nano-coatings was developed, enhancing hydrophilicity and antioxidant capacity. This modified mesh exhibits superior stability and biocompatibility, holding promise for hernia repair.

abstract: Dopant-controlled nanocluster assemblies exhibit tunable size and structure, revealing their potential in cancer theranostics through combined imaging and therapy.

abstract: In this study we present three different strategies to include poorly water-soluble drugs into biodegradable multilayer capsules. These approaches show promising outcome for the reduction of inflammation in inflamed cell models.

abstract: Molecular dynamics simulation assisted design of micellar drug delivery carrier and experimental validation.

abstract: Schematic illustration of the cytocompatibility and osteogenic activity mechanisms of the Ica-loaded on surface carbon fiber-reinforced polyetheretherketone.

abstract: This study developed Fe–30Mn– x Cu ( x = 0, 1, 4, 8) bio-alloys via microwave sintering. The alloys offer rapid degradation, favourable mechanical support, MRI compatibility, and biological functionality, enabled by their porous structure and Cu-rich phases.

abstract: A natural polymer-based composite hydrogel dressing with a dynamic and nondynamic synergy network was fabricated. Mechanical robustness as well as excellent self-healing and antibacterial properties were integrated into the hydrogel dressing.

abstract: The hydrogel membrane integrates barrier protection, autonomous mechanical stimulation, and osteogenesis-promoting inorganic microenvironment.

title: Polymers of intrinsic microporosity (PIMs) with immobilized carbonic anhydrase (CA) for extracorporeal membrane oxygenation
abstract: Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO) is widely used to save patients with severe respiratory failure.

title: Immunomodulatory and tumor-targeting palladium nanoparticles functionalized with resveratrol via green nanotechnology: synthesis, mechanisms, and in vivo therapeutic evaluation
abstract: Resveratrol functionalized palladium nanoparticles (Res-PdNPs) inhibit NF-κB, reprogram tumor-associated macrophages from the M2 to the M1 phenotype, and enhance in vivo anti-tumor activity.

abstract: Savinase-functionalized nanoparticles successfully enhanced the cytotoxic activity of ATO/D-VC in a 3D CRC cell culture, far exceeding the efficacy of both free drug and uncoated nanoparticle formulations.

abstract: The hydrated micelle coating, composed of hyaluronic acid and Pluronic, demonstrated superior hydrophilicity and aqueous lubricity to effectively alleviate mucosal lesions and inflammation caused by NGT insertion.

title: Tailoring diblock copolymers for efficient siPLK1 delivery and enhanced gene therapy of orthotopic osteosarcoma
abstract: The structure of the engineered polymer carrier BP 20 and its complexation with siRNA. Schematic illustration of BP 20 /siPLK1 nanoparticle (NP) mediated cellular internalization and PLK1 gene silencing in osteosarcoma (143B) cells.

abstract: Small amphiphilic gold nanoparticles with mixed ligand surfaces were loaded with antibiotics. The nanoparticles showed antimicrobial activity against Staphylococcus aureus and were able to penetrate biofilms and eradicate the bacteria.

title: Biomimetic semiconducting polymer dots for dual targeted NIR-II phototheranostic and multimodal coordinated immunostimulatory therapy
abstract: Breast cancer is a global health challenge necessitating more precise and effective treatment strategies.

title: Silicon dioxide layer thickness-dependent Au nanocube @mSiO 2 @Ag with surface enhanced Raman scattering for trace detection of harmful substances
abstract: Au nanocube @mSiO 2 @Ag nanocomposites were synthesized by layer-by-layer assembly with an Au nanocube core, an Ag shell, and mesoporous silica as a spacer.

title: PET-leveraged ALDH probe toward cancer stem cells
abstract: AldeCou-1 enables aldehyde dehydrogenase (ALDH)-mediated PET turn-off fluorescence for live imaging of ALDH overexpressed cancer cells.

title: Mesoporous polymeric nanoparticles for effective treatment of inflammatory diseases: an in vivo study
abstract: Glycine-based polymer nanoparticles for the treatment of inflammatory diseases without using any additional drugs.

title: Influence of hydrophilic/hydrophobic diols on the properties of polyurethane hydrogels: solvent-free one-pot synthesis
abstract: Bio-degradable porous polyurethane hydrogels by a green, solvent-free, one-pot technique that can withstand physiological mechanical loads and aid in tissue regeneration.

abstract: A flexible and cytotoxic drug carrier based on a porous supramolecular metal–organic material containing copper and adenine, which is loaded with cisplatin and 5-fluorouracil for combination cancer therapy.

title: Advanced 3D scaffolds for corneal stroma regeneration: a preclinical progress
