title: Transplantation of olfactory ensheathing cells embedded in the GelMA-F127DA hydrogel promotes the repair of spinal cord injury
abstract: The preparation method of the GelMA-F127DA hydrogel and the physiological changes and behavioral changes in rats resulting from the transplantation of OECs embedded in the GEF hydrogel into the SCI area are summarized. Figure was drawn by Figdraw.

title: QCM-based ammonia gas sensors with electrospun polymer-based nanofibers for liver and kidney disease detection: a mini-review
abstract: This review presents the recent advances in QCM-based ammonia gas sensors with electrospun polymeric nanofibers. Emphasizing their potential for liver and kidney disease diagnosis via breath analysis.

title: A photothermal therapy-based composite hydrogel for sequential management of inflammation control and bone regeneration in severe periodontitis
abstract: We developed an injectable hydrogel platform (PDMM@LF-HAMA), which can initially control inflammation through antibacterial effects, alleviate diffuse gingival bleeding and then promote bone regeneration with further photothermal therapy.

title: Balancing antibacterial activity and toxicity in silver-loaded hydroxyapatite: the impact of the silver nanoparticle incorporation method
abstract: Hydroxyapatite (HAp) possesses limited inherent antimicrobial activity, which can be significantly enhanced through the incorporation of silver nanoparticles (AgNPs) in a manner that minimizes cytotoxicity.

title: Modular molecular design of polymerized pro-estrogen materials enables controlled astrocyte response
abstract: Thermoplastic materials based on the polymerization of 17β-estradiol have demonstrated the ability to orchestrate astrocyte reactivity in vitro .

title: Nanozymes as next-generation ROS scavengers: design strategies, catalytic mechanisms, and therapeutic frontiers
abstract: ROS have dual roles in health. Antioxidant nanomaterials (vitamins, polyphenols, SeNPs, quantum dots, multi-enzyme systems, MOFs) show therapeutic promise for NAFLD, ALI, AKI, IBD, arthritis, and ischemia-reperfusion injury.

title: Stimuli-responsive core–shell–shell nanocarriers for implant-directed magnetic drug targeting
abstract: Magnetic-directable and dispersable core–shell–shell nanocarriers based on magnetic nanoporous silica nanoparticles functionalized with a pH-responsive polymer are suitable for the local pH-triggered delivery of an antibiotic.

title: Injection site-retained lipid nanoparticles for targeted intramuscular delivery of mRNA RSV prefusion-F vaccine
abstract: YK-201, YK-202, and YK-209 exhibit superior mRNA delivery efficiency both in vitro and in vivo and enable muscle-selective mRNA delivery, inducing robust humoral immune responses and exhibit good tolerance formulated with RSV-preF-mRNA vaccine.

title: Enhanced liquid–liquid phase separation of stress granules in a reconstructed model and their cytoplasmic targeting using a DNA nanodevice
abstract: The regulation of liquid–liquid phase separation in an in vitro reconstructed stress granule model is achieved by employing a triangular prism-shaped DNA nanostructure to spatially regulate the multivalency interactions between G3BP1 and total RNAs.

title: Utilizing an aqueous-liquid crystal interface to investigate membrane protein interactions and mutation effects of a pore-forming toxin
abstract: The cholesterol-recognition motif (CRM) aids Listeriolysin O (LLO) binding and activity at low cholesterol, which can be sensed by a biomimetic aqueous-liquid crystal interface.

title: Physically crosslinked agarose–hyaluronic acid hydrogel for injectable treatment of photoaged skin
abstract: An injectable agarose–hyaluronic acid composite hydrogel enhances skin hydration, restores dermal structure, and effectively repairs photoaged skin.

title: Engineered Ti 3 C 2 (O,Cl) MXenes with dual functionalization: a new Frontier in targeted head and neck squamous cell carcinoma and breast adenocarcinoma
abstract: Ti 3 C 2 (O,Cl) MXene, synthesized from Ti 3 AlC 2 in 73.7% yield, exhibits selective cytotoxicity vs. FaDu and MCF-7 cancer cell lines and triggers high ROS generation in cancer cells via oxygen-rich surface terminations, driving targeted oxidative damage.

title: Hemocompatible nucleosome-inspired heparin-mimicking hydrogel microspheres for safe and efficient extracorporeal removal of circulating histones in critically ill patients
abstract: The tailored RCHMs can adsorb circulating histones through electrostatic interactions with excellent hemocompatibility and significantly attenuate histone-mediated thrombocytopenia, platelet aggregation, and endothelial cell death.

title: Dopasomes: dopamine-mediated cross-linked lipid vesicles
abstract: Dopasomes are liposomes formed from self-polymerizing dopamine lipids, exhibiting robust stability through polydopamine cross-linking. They resist surfactants and drug leakage, offering a platform for next-generation lipid-based nanocarriers.

title: Bioinspired sodium alginate/silk fibroin dual-crosslinked conductive injectable hydrogel for neural stem cell therapy in cerebral palsy
abstract: Conductive injectable hydrogel loaded with neural stem cells for treatment of cerebral palsy.

title: Alleviation of myocardial infarction by hydrogen sulfide-releasing nanoparticles: mechanisms and therapeutic effects
abstract: Myocardial infarction (MI), a common and severe disease threatening human health worldwide, results from ischemic and hypoxic-induced necrosis of cardiac tissue due to coronary artery obstruction or rupture.

title: Correction: Development of a tannic acid- and silicate ion-functionalized PVA–starch composite hydrogel for in situ skeletal muscle repairing
abstract: Correction for ‘Development of a tannic acid- and silicate ion-functionalized PVA–starch composite hydrogel for in situ skeletal muscle repairing’ by Longkang Li et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2024, 12 , 3917–3926, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3TB03006G.

title: Enhanced sonodynamic therapy and theranostic integration for breast cancer treatment: nanomaterial-driven multifunctional platforms
abstract: This review covers sonodynamic therapy (SDT) nanomaterials for breast cancer, including both therapeutic and theranostic agents. It highlights ultrasound-guided strategies and discusses challenges for clinical translation.

title: Influence of material format and surface chemistry for the sustained delivery and efficacy of silk drug delivery systems in vivo
abstract: In this study, the influence of material format and surface chemistry on the drug release and effectivity were evaluated to give insights on how silk drug delivery systems can be improved based on the target application.

title: A TME-responsive oxygen-self-supplying hybridized polymersome for synergistic triple-modal therapy and precision theranostics in hypoxic tumors
abstract: IR808/DOX@Psome/MnO 2 as a pH/GSH dual-responsive nanotherapeutic platform for tumor hypoxia relief and synergistic therapy.

title: Artificial intelligence-enabled hydrogels: innovations and applications
abstract: AI accelerates the development process and design of hydrogels, predicts the properties and biocompatibility of hydrogels and boosts the multi-scenario bio-applications of hydrogels.

title: Dual-membrane bioinspired nanocarriers for targeted therapy of MRSA-induced acute lung injury and bacteremia
abstract: Bioinspired nanoparticles enhance the targeting of specific organs by facilitating interactions and communication at the nano-bio interface.

title: Quantitative features of osteo-bioactive Ti surfaces at the atomic/molecular level
abstract: Quantitative features of osteo-bioactive Ti surfaces at the atomic/molecular level.

title: Multifunctional NO supramolecular nanomedicine for thrombus risk reduction and intimal hyperplasia inhibition
abstract: Multifunctional supramolecular nanomedicine co-delivering NO donors and phenylboronic acid esters achieves synergistic thrombus reduction and intimal hyperplasia inhibition via targeted accumulation at vascular injury sites.

title: Hydrogen-bonded multi-mode liquid crystal elastomer actuators
abstract: We present multi-mode, supramolecular liquid crystal elastomer actuator that integrates shape memory programming, humidity sensitivity, and photochemical actuation, depending on the pre-activation conditions.

title: A step towards non-invasive diagnosis of diabetes mellitus using in situ synthesized MOF–MXene hybrid material with extended gate field-effect transistor integration
abstract: Integration of MOF-MXene hybrid material into an EGFET configuration for enzyme-free and non-invasive glucose detection to diagnose diabetes mellitus.

title: Parallel comparative studies on composition-dependent peroxidase-like catalytic activity of ultrasmall ferrite nanoparticles
abstract: Parallel comparative studies on composition-dependent peroxidase-like catalytic activity of ultrasmall CoFe 2 O 4 , MnFe 2 O 4 and γ-Fe 2 O 3 nanoparticles.

title: A dual-functional biodegradable composite coating fabricated on sulfonated PEEK via vacuum cold spraying: immunomodulation-driven osteointegration
abstract: A vacuum cold-sprayed bioactive coating converts inert PEEK into an immunomodulatory implant, synergistically enhancing bone integration through macrophage polarization and sustained ionic release.

title: Selective detection of an antibiotic using hydrogen-bonded organic frameworks (HOFs): insights from DFT mechanistic analysis
abstract: A mesoporous HOF nanostructure-based turn-off/turn-on fluorometric sensor for the selective detection of enrofloxacin in complex matrices.

title: Biomimetic lymph node-like scaffolds for optimized CAR-T cell expansion and potentiated antitumor efficacy
abstract: A biomimetic lymph node-like poriferous microsphere (PM) was developed to establish a 3D culture platform optimized for both T cell and CAR-T cell proliferation.

title: Thermally adaptive iohexol-loaded microcages for local computerized tomography
abstract: Temperature-responsive iohexol-loaded microcages demonstrated controlled expansion in a tumor microenvironment ( T > UCST), enabling enhanced local CT imaging.

title: Supramolecular avobenzone sunscreen: a promising method for enhancing UV protection and photostability
abstract: The damages caused by ultraviolet (UV) radiation to the skin have become a global public health concern.

title: Controllable fabrication of silk fibroin porous scaffolds and their regulation on cellular behaviours
abstract: Silk fibroin porous scaffolds with controllable pore size and mechanical properties were prepared and their effect on the regulation of the biological behavior of three types of cells was investigated.

title: Mechanically active biomaterials for tissue repair and regeneration
abstract: Bioinspired mechanoactive biomaterials transform stimuli into tunable mechanical forces, boosting tissue regeneration via shrinkable/expandable/massage-like dynamics.

title: A programmable shape memory bioscaffold incorporating doxorubicin for postoperative chemotherapy and enhanced bone regeneration
abstract: Development of shape memory scaffolds for localised chemotherapy and enhanced osteogenic regeneration after bone cancer resection.

title: Ultrasound-responsive smart biomaterials for bone tissue engineering
abstract: Bone defects resulting from trauma, tumors, or other injuries significantly impact human health and quality of life.

title: Spray-drying-engineered CS/HA-bilayer microneedles enable sequential drug release for wound healing
abstract: The CS/HA-bilayer microneedles with a core–shell structure were fabricated via solvent casting integrated with spray deposition, achieving rapid DOX release for antibacterial action while enabling sustained VEGF delivery to stimulate angiogenesis.

title: Development of a magnetically driven microrobot covered with a time-dependent film for colon drug delivery
abstract: The schematic illustration of motion and drug-sustained release of microrobot.

title: Going hi-res in bulk: flowless multiangle dynamic light scattering for detection on asymmetric flow field flow fractionation
abstract: Nanomedicine is an emerging field of research, demanding new analytical methods to respond to stringent regulatory and safety requirements. MADLS-AF4 emerges as a versatile and fit-for-purpose tool for accurate nanoparticle size determination.

title: Oxidation-responsive PEG-poly(α-lipoic acid) nanoparticles for coenzyme Q 10 delivery attenuate hepatic ischemia-reperfusion injury via ROS scavenging and ferroptosis inhibition
abstract: We have developed a versatile oxidation-responsive nanoparticle system that delivers coenzyme Q 10 to sites of tissue injury and achieves potent protection against hepatic ischemia-reperfusion injury.

title: Hierarchical biofilm models using sodium alginate beads containing bacteria embedded in a cellulose–chitosan hydrogel matrix
abstract: Introducing a novel 3D beads-in-hydrogel biofilm model that mimics natural biofilms, achieving high bacterial density from Day 1 and maintaining stability with sustained bacterial retention for 13 days, offering a robust tool for biofilm research.

title: Advances in peptide/polymer antimicrobial assemblies
abstract: This review highlights the importance of peptide/polymer nano-antimicrobial systems in improving antimicrobial activity, stability, cytotoxicity, and bioavailability.

title: Advances in three-dimensional hydrogel networks for cancer immunotherapy
abstract: Cancer immunotherapies have advanced treatment, but low response rates and side effects remain. 3D hydrogels offer new strategies to overcome these barriers, enhance immunotherapy, and improve outcomes. This review explores hydrogel-based immunotherapy advances and future directions.

title: Peptide-functionalized nanocapsules for targeted inhibition of β2-microglobulin amyloid aggregation
abstract: In this study, we developed a β2-microglobulin (β2m)-binding peptide (CF)-functionalized nanocomposite (NC-CF), which effectively binds to β2m via interactions with CF, thereby mitigating β2m aggregation and offering cellular protection.

title: Self-assembled nanomaterials enable extended lithium release
abstract: A novel extended-release lithium formulation to improve bipolar disorder treatment is reported. Lithium ion incorporation into metal-phenolic networks extends the release time fivefold and prevents toxic concentrations from being reached in serum.

title: Microstructure-tailored β-tricalcium phosphate scaffolds utilize degradation-guided osteoclast suppression to accelerate bone regeneration
abstract: β-Tricalcium phosphate (β-TCP) has been a valuable artificial bone material in clinical applications owing to its bioactivity, osteoconductivity, and degradability.

title: On-demand electrically controlled perampanel delivery from a PEDOT/SNP composite for seizure control
abstract: Perampanel-loaded porous SNPs were used as dopants for PEDOT films through electropolymerization. Perampanel is delivered directly to the implantation site as needed via a bidirectional rectangular waveform, which helps reduce seizure frequency.

title: A liquid metal-based sticky conductor for wearable and real-time electromyogram monitoring with machine learning classification
abstract: This study presents an LM–PDMS sticky conductor with high conductivity, flexibility, and strong adhesion, achieving 83.33% accuracy in EMG-based muscle movement classification, demonstrating its potential for real-time monitoring and rehabilitation.

title: Sprayed antibacterial layer on titanium implants to eradicate biofilms by photoelectrons under near-infrared light
abstract: A universal spray with pre-synthesized NIR-active nanoparticles in suspension, can be coated on anodized Ti implants, to remove biofilms using NIR treatment both in vitro and in vivo through an extracellular photoelectron transfer mechanism.

title: Regulation of the diabetic immune microenvironment by metformin-loaded strontium-doped mesoporous bioactive glass facilitates bone regeneration
abstract: Strontium-doped mesoporous bioactive glass loading metformin (MET/SrMBG) can be utilized to regulate chronic inflammation in the diabetic microenvironment, ultimately facilitating bone repair.

title: EGFR-targeted micelles-in-lipopolymersome nanocarriers for overcoming drug resistance in triple-negative breast cancer
abstract: Micelles-in-lipopolymersome nanocarriers combine structural stability and targeted drug delivery to treat drug-resistant triple-negative breast cancer.

title: Synergistic integration of ADSCs and egg white hydrogel microspheres for accelerated wound regeneration
abstract: Bioactive microcarriers (Bio-MCs) formulated by egg white hydrogel microspheres and ADSCs enhancing cellular function and wound healing through growth factor-mediated paracrine signaling.

title: Targeted drug delivery systems for rheumatoid arthritis: advancing precision medicine in autoimmune therapies
abstract: Nanocarrier-mediated multi-level precision delivery overcomes biological barriers and enables stimuli-responsive targeting at the cellular and subcellular levels, offering transformative therapeutic potential for autoimmune diseases.

title: Synergistic healing of diabetic wounds through photothermal and peroxidase-like activity of heterogeneous Bi 2 S 3 /Au nanoparticles
abstract: Bi 2 S 3 /Au NPs display highly efficient photothermal conversion efficiency and POD-like enzyme activity. This study provides a promising solution for innovative therapy of refractory diabetic wounds.

title: A pH-responsive fluorescence nanosystem for imaging lipid deposition in diseased aortic valves
abstract: A lysosome acidic pH responsive nanosystem was prepared for the delivery of a lipid droplet-specific probe ECNBD to illuminate the deposited lipids in the diseased heart valves (DHVs), confirming the feasibility of DHVs-targeted drug delivery.

title: In situ dressing based on a D–π–A structured aggregation-induced emission photosensitizer for healing infected wounds
abstract: A new photodynamic antibacterial dressing was developed by employing a highly efficient photosensitizer, DTTPB, which could efficiently both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria without inducing obvious phototoxicity in mammalian cells.

title: A dendritic drug–drug conjugate self-assembled hypoxia-responsive supramolecular nanoparticle for combination therapy
abstract: Based on a dendritic drug–drug conjugate, a hypoxia-responsive supramolecular nanoparticle SN@IR806-CB was constructed via the synergy of π–π stacking interaction, host–guest complexation, and hydrophobic interactions for synergistic therapies.

title: Nanozyme-mediated high-entropy-driven photothermally enhanced tumor catalytic therapy
abstract: A high entropy Prussian blue analogue significantly potentiated the efficacy of catalytic therapy.

title: The synthesis and optical properties of Cu–In–S/ZnS nanocrystals in buffer solution for near-infrared fluorescence imaging
abstract: In this report, we describe the synthesis of infrared emitting CuInS 2 /ZnS quantum dots in buffer solution, the role of the buffer and the use of the particles in imaging.

title: Ligand-dependent activity engineering of Cu-MOFs based on biomimetic strategies for nanozyme-decorated smart hydrogels for therapy of inflammatory bone defects
abstract: Reconstructing inflammatory bone defects using stem cell-based therapy faces challenges posed by the oxidative stress microenvironment.

title: Efficient red-emitting carbon dots and albumin composites for precise synovial bioimaging in rheumatoid arthritis
abstract: Based on the high affinity between BSA and SPARC secreted in the synovium of CIA rats, BSA is utilized to efficiently deliver red-emitting carbon dots for targeted fluorescence imaging of the lesion joints in CIA rats.

title: Progress in Chinese medicine monomers and their nanoformulations on myocardial ischemia/reperfusion injury
abstract: The aim of this review is to elucidate the pathomechanism of MIRI and present the latest developments in the use of TCM monomers and nanomaterials in combination with TCM monomers for the treatment of MIRI.

title: Waste-derived marigold flower carbon dot spray and gel formulations exhibit enhanced wound healing in deep excisional cutaneous and burn wounds
abstract: Bioactive waste derived carbon dots have shown significant progress in biomedical therapeutics due to their unique physicochemical characteristics and exceptional biocompatibility.

title: Natural polymer-based soft actuators: from biomass to bioapplications
abstract: Nature-inspired soft actuators constructed from polymers have been widely used in wearables, implants, and soft robotics.

title: A cascaded amplification carrier-free nanoplatform for synergistic photothermal/ferroptosis therapy via dual antioxidant pathway disruption in cervical cancer
abstract: Dual-laser triggered carrier-free self-assembly nano-photosensitizers (Ir-TCF 3 P-FA NPs) with trimodal imaging performance significantly inhibited the progression of human cervical cancer through synergistic photothermal/ferroptosis therapy.

title: Sensing of lung cancer biomarkers using titanium carbide (Ti 2 C) MXenes
abstract: Functionalized Ti 2 C MXenes are promising for non-invasive lung cancer detection. Their low work function and high sensitivity to VOCs like aniline make them ideal for breath sensors.

title: Proteome and microbiome profiles of polymicrobial salivary biofilms on 3D MEW fibrous scaffolds: biomimetic ECM-inspired structures
abstract: (a) Schematic of the melt electrowritten mPCL scaffold. (b) Healthy salivas were inoculated on 2D TCP and 3D MEW mPCL scaffolds; microbial composition was analyzed by 16S rRNA sequencing, and proteomic profiles by LC-MS/MS; created with Biorender.

title: Advances in drug delivery systems based on liposome-composite hydrogel microspheres
abstract: Research on liposome-composite hydrogel microspheres (LHMs) drug delivery systems, primarily composed of drugs, liposomes, and hydrogels, has garnered growing scientific interest.

title: Polymeric nanocarriers for cancer treatment: the promise of sensitive poly(2-(diisopropylamino)ethyl methacrylate)
abstract: Poly(2-(diisopropylamino)ethyl methacrylate) is a pH-responsive polymer and its copolymers can form nanosystems for the treatment of different types of cancer through gene therapy, drug delivery or photodynamic therapy.

title: Anti-neoangiogenic nanodelivery systems: advances in tumor-based and ophthalmic disease research
abstract: Nanoplatform-based strategies for anti-angiogenic therapy are discussed.

title: Calcium polyphosphate endows silk fibroin hydrogels with enhanced osteogenesis for bone function restoration
abstract: We introduce amorphous calcium polyphosphate (CaPP) with tunable degrees of polymerization (DP) as a bioactive component into regenerated silk fibroin (RSF) hydrogels to address the bone regeneration challenge.

title: Cu-doped Co 9 S 8− x sonozymes for enhanced sonodynamic and chemodynamic therapy of gallbladder cancer through ion doping and vacancy engineering
abstract: The ROS production amplification strategy was reported through ion doping and vacancy engineering, which can not only improve the sonodynamic activity of sonosensitizers but also enhance the chemodynamic properties of nanozymes.

title: Innovative dual-contrast nanocoating for central venous catheters: prolonged infection resistance and enhanced imaging
abstract: CVCs coated with laccase–manganese phosphate hybrid nanostructures effectively prevent bacterial infections with high biocompatibility and in vivo safety.

title: Efficient release of COS/H 2 S via BODIPY-based photocaged thionocarbamates
abstract: BODIPY-based thionocarbamates photo-uncage 100× faster than carbamates, due to the intrinsic reactivity of thiocarbonyls. These scaffolds quickly release COS/H 2 S in biological settings, highlighting potential applications in H 2 S-related therapies.

title: Tailoring the synthesis of V 0.25 (Zr 1.75 )C MXene for sensitive SERS quantification of ciprofloxacin antibiotics: spectroscopic and DFT investigation
abstract: Synthesis protocol followed to prepare silver nanosphere-loaded bimetallic MXene AgNS@V 0.25 (Zr 1.75 )CT x from its MAX phase precursor.

title: A customizable 32 P hydrogel applicator for brachytherapy of skin hemangioma based on machine learning and 3D-printing
abstract: This work develops a customizable 32P hydrogel applicator for brachytherapy of skin hemangioma based on machine learning and 3D-printing, which demonstrates great practical potentials and can inspire the design of precision medical system.

title: Enhanced osteo–angiogenic coupling by a bioactive cell-free fat extract (CEFFE) delivered through electrospun fibers
abstract: Fat extract (FE) components released from the FE-PDA@PCL/GT fiber mesh promoted paracrine signaling communication between osteoblastic and endothelial cells. This interaction together with FE, contributed to the enhanced osteo–angiogenic coupling.

title: Imparting new stimuli-responsive behaviors in protein–polymers via self-immolative linker conjugation
abstract: Conjugation of self-immolative molecules imparts H 2 O 2 - or β-galactosidase-responsiveness to protein-based polymers, enabling the creation of stimuli-responsive nanostructures and hydrogels.

title: Recent advances in polymer-based thin-film electrodes for ECoG applications
abstract: This review evaluated electrocorticography (ECoG) electrode performance based on key properties and improvements from polymers, categorizing them as sensitive or structural, while discussing preparation methods, challenges, and future perspectives.

title: Atomically dispersed copper( i ) on tungstosilicic acid for catalytic protection against cisplatin-induced hearing loss
abstract: Cu SAN attenuates cisplatin-induced ototoxicity by inhibiting cuproptosis via directly decreasing the production of ROS and inhibiting the expression of FDX1.

title: Dual-responsive polydopamine-embellished Zn-MOFs enabling synergistic photothermal and antibacterial metal ion therapy for oral biofilm eradication
abstract: This study introduces a simple, safe antibacterial strategy using polydopamine-coated Zn-MOF nanoparticles (ZIF-8@PDA NPs) to eradicate oral biofilms via synergistic photothermal and metal ion therapy.

title: A multifunctional sericin hydrogel system fabricated via one-step acetic acid induction for wound management
abstract: Hydrogels, particularly multifunctional hydrogels, are attractive wound dressings due to their abundant exudate absorption performance, oxygen permeability, comparable moisturizing ability, and performance of locally delivering therapeutic drugs.

title: Hydrogel-based ocular drug delivery systems
abstract: This review discussed the development of hydrogel-based ocular drug delivery devices.

title: Frontiers in fluorescence imaging: tools for the in situ sensing of disease biomarkers
abstract: A comprehensive overview of recent advancements in fluorescence imaging techniques for in situ sensing of various biomarkers, emphasizing the transformative potential of artificial intelligence in shaping the future of bioimaging.

title: Engineered nanomedicine targets liver cancer stem cells to treat liver cancer disease
abstract: Liver cancer stem cells (LCSCs) are a population of cells with self-renewal and self-differentiation capacities, widely recognized as critical for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) development.

title: Supramolecular eutectogel based on THDESs as a skin penetrating carrier for effective transdermal delivery of hydrophobic drug curcumin
abstract: Stimuli-responsive supramolecular eutectogels derived from THDESs for enhanced transdermal delivery of curcumin, highlighting their potential as innovative therapeutic soft materials.

title: Surface-enhanced Raman scattering for HSP 70A mRNA detection in live cells using silica nanoparticles and DNA-modified gold nanoparticles
abstract: Real-time monitoring of mRNA in living cells is crucial for understanding dynamic biological processes.

title: Correction: Polymeric nanocarriers for cancer treatment: the promise of sensitive poly(2-(diisopropylamino)ethyl methacrylate)
abstract: Correction for ‘Polymeric nanocarriers for cancer treatment: the promise of sensitive poly(2-(diisopropylamino)ethyl methacrylate)’ by Joana S. Ferreira et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2025, 13 , 7000–7013, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TB00268K.

title: Matching drug and polymer for efficient delivery of anti-inflammatory drugs: PLGA, polyesteramides, and acetalated dextran
abstract: Polymeric nanoparticles from PLGA, polyesteramides, and acetalated dextran enabled efficient delivery of anti-inflammatory drugs, highlighting the importance of polymer–drug matching for optimal formulation performance.

title: Bimetal phenolic network-based alginate cryogels for rapid hemostasis and photothermal enhanced bimetallic chemodynamic therapy for wound infection
abstract: This research developed a sodium alginate-based cryogel with Fe 3+ /Cu 2+ -bimetallic phenolic networks that synergizes photothermal/chemodynamic antibacterial therapy while promoting angiogenesis and wound healing.

title: Injectable thermosensitive hydrogel-encapsulated paclitaxel liposomes enriched with miR-455-3p antagomir for dormancy therapy of salivary adenoid cystic carcinoma
abstract: Lip@miR hydrogel delivered antagomiR-455-3p to awake dormant SACC cells, suppressing their tumorigenic potential and leading to their eradication through sustained paclitaxel release.

title: Development and application of hydrogels in pathogenic bacteria detection in foods
abstract: This review explores hydrogels as versatile materials for detecting foodborne pathogens, emphasizing their role in fast, sensitive, and specific bacteria detection through various sensing mechanisms.

title: Gadolinium-doped carbon dots derived from peanut shell waste for bioimaging applications
abstract: Carbon dots synthesized from peanut shell biomass and doped with gadolinium exhibit significant potential as efficient MRI contrast agents.

title: Cellulose microfiber-mediated mesoscale architecture promotes the expansion of patient-derived lung cancer organoids while preserving their malignant characteristics
abstract: MF-gel weaves cellulose microfibers into gelatin hydrogel to recapitulate the structural architecture of natural collagen fiber bundles, expanding patient lung cancer organoids while preserving invasion, histopathology, mutations and drug response.

title: Nanocarbon dots enhancing photothermal therapy for cancer: mechanisms, synergistic strategies, and frontier advances
abstract: Nanocarbon dots, as highly efficient photothermal conversion agents, continue to play a significant role in the field of cancer photothermal therapy.

title: Advances in immunomodulatory microneedles for diabetic wound healing
abstract: Microneedles (MNs) offer a minimally invasive transdermal platform for immunomodulation. This review summarizes recent advances in immunomodulatory MN strategies for diabetic wound healing.

title: A 3D printed multilayer biomimetic scaffold with a gradient-oriented structure for articular cartilage repair
abstract: A multilayer cartilage scaffold with a gradient pore size and directional arrangement structure was constructed using 3D bio-printing and template-freezing orientation technology to achieve a high degree of mimicry of the natural cartilage structure.

title: From simple delivery to multimodal systems: the critical role of macromolecular platforms in bioorthogonal drug synthesis
abstract: This review highlights the integration of bioorthogonal catalysis into macromolecular scaffolds to enable localized, in situ bond-forming reactions for prodrug activation, offering enhanced specificity and support for multimodal functionalities.

title: Nanomaterials and clinical SERS technology: broad applications in disease diagnosis
abstract: In this review, we highlight the advancements in clinical SERS diagnostics, the role of NMs-SERS and AI in enhancing SERS data analysis for precise diagnosis of chronic diseases and improving detection sensitivity, specificity, and reproducibility.

title: CNC-mediated functionalized MWCNT-reinforced double-network conductive hydrogels as smart, flexible strain and epidermic sensors for human motion monitoring
abstract: A novel flexible strain- and epidermis-sensitive sensor was designed using a cellulose nanocrystal (CNC)-mediated acid functionalized multiwalled carbon nanotube (A-MWCNT)-reinforced double-network conductive hydrogel.

title: Plant-derived exosome-like nanoparticles in tissue repair and regeneration
abstract: This article reviews the extraction and characterization of ELNs, discussing their structure-function relationship. It examines recent advances in ELNs for tissue regeneration and highlights challenges and future directions in tissue repair and regeneration.

title: Biodegradable semiconducting polymer nanoparticles for phototheranostics
abstract: This review summarizes recent advances in biodegradable semiconducting polymer nanoparticles for phototheranostics, such as tumor imaging, lymph node imaging, as well as phototherapy, photo-immunotherapy, and anti-infection therapy.

title: Targeting and structural engineering of light-responsive nanoprobes for hierarchical therapy: construction, optimization, and applications in cancer stem cells
abstract: Dual-targeted nanoparticles are designed for synergistic PTT and PDT of cancer stem cells. By improving the hypoxic environment and applying a hierarchical therapy strategy, these nanoparticles show enhanced therapeutic efficacy.

title: Synthesis and study of amorphous calcium phosphate dual-targeted drug-carrying platforms
abstract: A porous DS–HEP/ACP@AT drug delivery platform that effectively reaches atherosclerotic lesions to release therapeutic agents through passive and active targeting mechanisms.

title: Smartphone-integrated colorimetric and fluorometric sensor based on copper-doped carbon dots for visual and rapid detection of thiram
abstract: Achieving highly selective visual detection of thiram is essential for ensuring food safety and environmental protection.

title: Actively targeted hyperbranched metal organic framework-based core–shell nanocarriers for the treatment of breast cancer
abstract: Illustration of the hyperbranched nanocarrier, depicting the detailed core–shell chemical architecture, the physicochemical characterizations implemented, the intracellular biotargets of the released drug and representative in vivo biological data.

title: Breast cancer extracellular matrix invasion depends on local mechanical loading of the collagen network
abstract: Magnetic alginate beads coated with breast cancer cells were attracted to an external magnet, creating a local force that influenced invasion. Cancer cells around the bead proliferated ∼25% more upon magnet removal and stress relaxation.

title: Hybrid photo-crosslinked decellularized extracellular matrix scaffold from porcine Achilles tendon: a biocompatible and non-immunogenic matrix for tissue engineering
abstract: Decellularised porcine Achilles tendon-derived, photocrosslinkable extracellular matrix hydrogel.

title: Introduction to bioinspired functional supramolecular systems
abstract: Guest Editors João Borges, Patricia Y. W. Dankers, João F. Mano and Sébastien Lecommandoux introduce a Journal of Materials Chemistry B themed collection presenting the latest developments in the field of bioinspired functional supramolecular systems.

title: Lanthanum-loaded ZIF nanomaterials for caries prevention
abstract: Lanthanum-loaded ZIF nanoparticles enable sustained lanthanum release and effective enamel remineralization, offering a biocompatible, fluoride-free strategy for caries prevention with efficacy comparable to traditional fluoride treatments.

title: A multifunctional IL@MOF composite-based hydrogel for enhanced transdermal drug delivery of 5-fluorouracil
abstract: Advances in biomaterial-based transdermal drug delivery systems (TDDSs) are unlocking new possibilities for cancer therapy by enhancing skin permeability and enabling sustained drug release while minimizing systemic side effects.

title: Topical siRNA therapy of diabetic-like wound healing
abstract: The biocompatible nanofiber wound dressing enables the controlled release of the antisense siRNA against matrix metalloproteinase 9. In a diabetic-like murine model, the treatment resulted in a significant shortening of the wound healing time.

title: New thermoplastic poly(ester–ether) elastomers with enhanced mechanical properties derived from long-chain dicarboxylic acid for medical device applications
abstract: A thermoplastic elastomer with excellent toughness, biocompatibility, and remarkable elastic recovery was developed by incorporation of a bio-based long chain dicarboxylic acid via regulation of both hard and soft segments of poly(ester–ether).

title: Smart polydopamine nanodot-knotted hydrogels for photodynamic tumor therapy
abstract: Integrating additionally empowered polyphenol-based nanodots into a multifunctional hydrogel system via iminoboronates has achieved powerful PDT efficacy against tumors.

title: Competitive electrochemical assay of CA19-9 on gold nanoparticles/porous carbons nanohybrid amplified by polyoxometalate/aptamer/graphene quantum dot-decorated gold nanotags
abstract: Sensitive detection of CA19-9 using a competitive electrochemical assay on gold nanoparticles/porous carbons and amplification by polyoxometalate/aptamer/graphene quantum dot-decorated gold nanotags for gastric and pancreatic cancer diagnostics.

title: ZIF-8-based nanomaterials for diabetic wound healing: mechanisms, applications, and future perspectives
abstract: ZIF-8 nanocrystals modulate diabetic wound healing through antibacterial action, oxidative stress reduction, and immune regulation. They promote angiogenesis by enhancing endothelial cell migration and vascular remodeling in damaged tissue.

title: Carbon-based nanomaterials with higher specific surface area: more expensive but more effective antimicrobials
abstract: Titanium dental implants are prone to infections by pathogenic bacteria, leading to the formation of biofilm and development of peri-implantitis.

title: Towards cell-adhesive, 4D printable PCL networks through dynamic covalent chemistry
abstract: Photopolymerised 4D printable PCL networks using imine based dynamic covalent chemistry exhibiting glass transition-based shape memory with over 90% shape fixity and recovery, active cell adhesion, and scavenging reactive oxygen species.

title: In situ fabricated gold nanostars on hydrogel beads as photo-oxidase mimics for rapid and sustainable POCT of uric acid
abstract: Harnessing the photo-oxidase like activity of gold nanostars embedded on the surface of hydrogel macro-beads, a new point-of-care testing platform have been developed for accurate measurement of uric acid in bio-fluids.

title: Ultrasensitive detection of arsenic in water using laser-scribed graphene-based electrodes
abstract: Metal oxide nanoparticle incorporation via photothermal conversion of metal salts enables the catalytic activity of graphene towards heavy metal detection.

title: Engineering therapeutic scaffolds: integrating drug delivery with tissue regeneration
abstract: Our review presents a timely and comprehensive analysis of drug-eluting scaffolds for tissue regeneration, with particular emphasis on advanced controlled release strategies to optimize therapeutic outcomes.

title: A stable dual functional superhydrophobic coating to inhibit Proteus mirabilis colonization, migration, and encrustation formation for urinary catheter applications
abstract: A superhydrophobic coating with dual functionalities and stabilities against prolonged incubation and various frictions was developed. It exhibited excellent capabilities against Proteus mirabilis colonization, migration, and encrustation formation.

title: Effect of surface-engineered AuNPs on gene expression, bacterial interaction, protein denaturation, and toxicology assay: an in vitro and in vivo model
abstract: The role of non-toxic and highly biocompatible pamoic acid functionalized gold nanoparticles (PA@AuNPs) on in vitro gene expression, bacterial interaction, protein denaturation, and in vivo toxicology is assessed.

title: Exploiting supercooled medium-chain lipids and surfactant combinations for solid-phase dominant nanostructured lipid carrier production
abstract: Strategic surfactant selection and crystallization control improves drug loading, processing and stability. This approach enables controlled delivery and manufacturing applications.

title: Construction of a sandwich-type DNA biosensor based on functionalized MOF@COF nanomaterials for the detection of NSCLC biomarker ctDNA
abstract: An ultrasensitive sandwich electrochemical biosensor for NSCLC ctDNA detection employing a P-NG-AuNPs substrate and a MOF@COF@MB@AuNPs tracer label.

title: Recent progress in ZIF–polymer composites for advanced drug delivery applications
abstract: This review article highlights recent advances in ZIF–polymer composites for drug delivery, emphasizing their applications across diverse routes including pulmonary, ocular, transdermal, gastrointestinal, and ortho delivery.

title: Dye-sensitized lanthanide-doped upconversion nanoprobes for homocysteine sensing in human serum and living cells via a spatial optimization strategy
abstract: A dye-sensitized upconversion nanoprobe was developed for the specific detection of Hcy. It exhibits excellent sensitivity and rapid response and has been applied to the quantitative detection of Hcy in serum as well as cell-specific imaging.

title: Zeolitic imidazolate frameworks enhanced transfection efficiency of mRNA loaded lipid nanoparticles
abstract: Zeolitic imidazolate framework encapsulation of mRNA-loaded lipid nanoparticles significantly enhance transfection efficiency, offering a promising strategy for next-generation mRNA therapeutics.

title: Conducting composite scaffolds for antibacterial drug release and in situ electrochemical monitoring of bacterial growth
abstract: Exploring conducting polymer/MXene composite scaffolds loaded with antibacterial drugs enables controlled drug release and real-time electrochemical monitoring of bacterial growth, advancing next-generation antibacterial bioelectronic platforms.

title: Lactose-functionalized photosensitizer nanoparticles derived from thiophene-fused BODIPY for NIR-imaging-guided photodynamic treatment of hepatocellular carcinoma
abstract: Four near-infrared photosensitizers ( THTB , TBrTB , PHTB , and PBrTB ) based on thiophene-fused, BODIPY-conjugated, liver tumor-targeting lactose were synthesized. These photosensitizers generate ROS in hepatocellular carcinoma and induce apoptosis.

title: Nanomaterials in stimulus-responsive drug delivery systems facilitate precise therapy for hematologic diseases
abstract: Stimulus-responsive drug delivery systems release drugs in response to specific microenvironments or signal receptors, triggering targeted physiological reactions and offering precise therapeutic strategies for hematologic diseases with enhanced efficacy and reduced side effects.

title: Accelerated dermal wound healing in diabetic mice by a H 2 O 2 -generating catechol-functionalized gelatin microgel
abstract: A catechol-modified gelatin microgel transformed into a wound-covering adhesive film upon hydration. Oxygen in the aqueous solution oxidizes catechol to generate hydrogen peroxide as a byproduct, which accelerated wound healing and killed bacteria.

title: Osteoclastogenesis-characterized osteoinductive biphasic calcium phosphate ceramic for bone regeneration in rabbit maxillary sinus lift
abstract: Osteoclastogenesis in vitro and ectopic bone formation reflect regenerative potential in maxillary sinus lift, serving as predictive indicators of biomaterials for clinical bone regeneration.

title: A state-of-the-art review of multi-cross-linked hydrophobic associated hydrogels for soft electronic, biomedical, and environmental applications
abstract: Thanks to their considerable toughness, self-recoverability, high swelling degree and stimuli-responsiveness, hydrophobic association (HA) hydrogels are promising for wearable electronics, biomedical applications and the water treatment industry.

title: Mechanical strain-regulated hydrogel biodegradation for biological scaffolds with programmable lifetime
abstract: Mechanical strain is introduced as a programmable cue to regulate hydrogel degradation in situ , enabling next-generation scaffold development for controlled drug release and tissue remodeling.

title: Molecularly imprinted polymer-integrated nanozymes for biosensing: advances and prospects
abstract: Nanozyme@MIP composites merge catalytic nanozymes with specific molecularly imprinted polymers. This synergy creates biosensors with superior selectivity, sensitivity, and stability for diverse analytes and platforms.

title: Moiré superlattices of copper nanocluster assemblies for cancer theranostics
abstract: Chemically twisted stacking of two-dimensional copper nanocluster assemblies with moiré superlattices shows superior effects in cancer theranostics.

title: Expression of concern: Reduced graphene oxide nanosheets decorated with AuPd bimetallic nanoparticles: a multifunctional material for photothermal therapy of cancer cells
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Reduced graphene oxide nanosheets decorated with AuPd bimetallic nanoparticles: a multifunctional material for photothermal therapy of cancer cells’ by Gitashree Darabdhara et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2015, 3 , 8366–8374, https://doi.org/10.1039/C5TB01704A.

title: Phycocyanin functionalized selenium nanoparticles for type I photodynamic antibacterial therapy and wound healing
abstract: Photodynamic therapy (PDT) has emerged as a promising treatment for drug-resistant bacterial infections by avoiding the abuse of antibiotics.

title: Double network hydrogels encapsulating genetically modified dedifferentiated chondrocytes for auricular cartilage regeneration
abstract: A schematic representation of preparation of genetically modified dedifferentiated chondrocytes and porous double network hydrogels for their application in constructing tissue-engineered auricular cartilage scaffolds.

title: Split-type photoelectrochemical biosensing for miRNA-144 assay based on a ZnIn 2 S 4 /TiO 2 heterojunction
abstract: Due to the favorable band energy alignment, ZnIn 2 S 4 /TiO 2 heterojunctions with enhanced charge transfer and charge carrier separation were hydrothermally synthesized for the construction of a split-type photoelectrochemical biosensor for miRNA-144.

title: A bioactive Cu-grafted gel coating with micro–nano structures for simultaneous enhancement of bone regeneration and infection resistance
abstract: To prevent PJI, PVA–TP–CA/Cu@TC4 was obtained by constructing a micro–nano structure on TC4 and loading a PVA gel coating, which exhibited enhanced biofunctional properties, including antibacterial, angiogenesis, and osteogenic activities.

title: Charge engineering controls cooperative assembly and loading in protein host–guest complexes
abstract: Controlling cargo loading in self-assembling protein capsules remains a key challenge in supramolecular chemistry.

title: Multi-component SiO 2 –P 2 O 5 –Na 2 O–K 2 O–CaO–SrO–ZnO–CaF 2 –SrF 2 bioactive glass with a lower fluoride content can form strontium-containing fluorapatite: a 19 F MAS-NMR analysis
abstract: Low-fluoride glasses formed strontium-containing fluorapatite (Sr-FAp), while higher-fluoride glasses formed Ca 0.5 Sr 0.5 F 2 , guiding future formulations for bioactive dental materials.

title: Molecular-weight-engineered PLA composite inks for room-temperature 3D printing of high-fidelity osteogenic scaffolds
abstract: This study presents a molecular-weight-engineered strategy for room-temperature 3D printing of osteogenic PLA/PCL/n-HA scaffolds, where PLA's molecular weight ( M w ) governs printability and structural fidelity.

title: Leveraging nanomaterials for ultrasensitive biosensors in early cancer detection: a review
abstract: Recent advances in nanotechnology revolutionize cancer diagnostics, better than PET-scans and biopsies. Ultrasensitive nanobiosensors spot trace biomarkers effectively, allowing early, precise detection and better outcomes, altering cancer care globally.

title: Citrus pectin-coated inhalable PLGA nanoparticles for treatment of pulmonary fibrosis
abstract: Citrus pectin coated PLGA nanoparticles can significantly ameliorate pulmonary fibrosis over 24–72 h in vivo .

title: Electrospun anisotropic fiber reinforced composites for artificial heart valves
abstract: Anisotropic composite valves that approximate natural heart valves are essential for the successful construction of tissue-engineered heart valves.

title: Architecting a partial thickness cartilage substitute with mimetic, self-assembling hydrogels
abstract: A self-adhesive bilayered implant mimicking the stiffness, strength, and hydration of superficial-transitional zones of articular cartilage. This design leverages triple network hydrogels with polyelectrolyte and polyampholyte networks.

title: Non-Ti MXenes: new biocompatible and biodegradable candidates for biomedical applications
abstract: This review discusses the properties of non-Ti MXenes, such as biocompatibility, biodegradability, antibacterial activity, and neuroprotective effects, highlighting their potential in various biomedical applications in comparison with Ti MXenes.

title: Pt( iv )-functionalised polyacrylic acid-coated iron oxide magnetic nanoparticles as redox-responsive cancer theranostics
abstract: A novel redox-responsive theranostic nanocomposite was developed to deliver a platinum prodrug to solid tumors.

title: Comprehensive overview of biomaterials for bone regeneration in cleft lip and palate
abstract: The left part presents a schematic of maxillofacial tissue development (derived from neural crest cells) and the pathogenesis of alveolar cleft in CL/P, while the right part outlines the status of biomaterials for bone regeneration in CL/P.

title: Hydrogel microneedles with multifunctional strategy for prolonged hyperuricemia management
abstract: An efficient and long-term stable non-oral drug delivery system is preferable for treating hyperuricemia, as it can minimize adverse effects and prolong treatment efficacy. Herein, the hydrogel microneedles (HMNs) system...

title: Soft nanoparticles incorporating a benzo[ c ]xanthene fluorophore: facile synthesis and ratiometric pH sensing
abstract: A holistic approach to the development of a platform to trace the intracellular fate of miktoarm polymer based soft nanoparticles, centered around the ratiometric pH sensor SNAFL-C, is described.

title: Theoretical framework for the kinetics and thermodynamics of cargo loading into affinity hydrogels
abstract: Despite significant attention directed toward affinity hydrogels as promising platforms for the controlled storage of therapeutic molecular cargo, the loading process remains incompletely understood.

title: Waterborne dispersion-processed self-healing elastomers with hydrogen-bond locked hydrophobic microdomains for multifunctional applications
abstract: This paper reports a novel self-healing WPU elastomer prepared via the aqueous dispersion method, which is endowed with multifunctional properties through hydrogen-bond locked hydrophobic microdomains.

title: Advanced nanoparticle platform for targeted pancreatic cancer therapy: optimized gemcitabine delivery with biomimetic coatings
abstract: Engineered Fullgem particles selectively target PANC-1 cells via dynamin-mediated endocytosis, enabling safer drug delivery through membrane coating and fluorescence-based tracking.

title: Biodegradable Zn–Li–Sr alloy implants with antioxidant properties for improved bone osseointegration under diabetic conditions
abstract: Fabrication and characterization of biodegradable Zn–Li–Sr alloy for enhanced diabetic bone defect repair.

title: Durable and breathable ZnO/graphene-coated fabrics for enhanced BQR protection
abstract: This work details a new strategy for active CBRN protection based on polyurethane-based coatings providing dual fabric functionalization: outer (water/oil repellency) & inner (CBRN protection).

title: A glucose-responsive alginate-based hydrogel laden with modified GLP-1 and telmisartan ameliorates type 2 diabetes and reduces liver and kidney toxicities
abstract: The developed injectable glucose-responsive hydrogel (Diabogel) presents a promising approach for regulating blood glucose levels, restoring pancreatic β cell function, and reducing hepatic and renal toxicities.

title: RGDS-modified ursolic acid: insights into self-assembly, nano-properties, osteoporosis inhibition and molecular mechanisms
abstract: UA-RGDS was capable of simultaneously preventing bone loss and downregulating the expression of IL-1β in the serum. Thus, serum IL-1β is a potential biomarker for clinically monitoring the bone health of menopausal women.

title: Dual crosslinked injectable hydrogels with phytic acid coordination for robust underwater adhesion and rapid hemostasis
abstract: The PA-coordinated injectable hydrogel exhibits a high swelling ratio, stable adhesiveness, a low hemolysis rate, and good cytocompatibility, holding promising potential for rapid hemostasis of internal organs under minimally invasive conditions.

title: Two photon-responsive gold nanocapsules enable targeted photothermal hyperthermia of chemoresistant melanoma: injection-route-dependent efficacy and renal evidence of fragment clearance
authors: M. Carmen Iglesias de la Cruz
abstract: Gold–polymer nanocapsules enable image-guided, intratumoural photothermal therapy of melanoma. Intrinsic properties allow for two-photon luminescence imaging prior to local heating.

title: Metal–organic frameworks as anchors for giant unilamellar vesicle immobilization
abstract: Here we demonstrate the most reliable way to immobilize giant unilamellar vesicles (GUVs) using metal-organic frameworks (MOFs).

title: Astragalus polysaccharide-containing 3D-printed scaffolds promote cartilage regeneration by correcting metabolic disorders and promoting cellular matrix remodeling
abstract: Astragalus polysaccharide-containing 3D-printed scaffolds show great potential for cartilage defect repair.

title: Bioactive glasses and polyphenols: towards synergistic biological effects for tissue regeneration
abstract: This review presents the strategic coupling of bioactive glasses with polyphenols for tissue engineering, detailing factors governing their interaction and highlighting promising in vitro and in vivo therapeutic results.

title: Self-assembling peptide nanomaterials co-engineered with linoleic acid and catechol motifs for synergistic ferroptosis–photothermal therapy–chemotherapy
abstract: Self-assembling hybrid nanomaterials with synergistic ferroptosis–photothermal–chemotherapy properties were prepared from the conjugate of a linoleic acid moiety and a peptide segment with the catechol group.

title: BSA/PEI/GOD modified cellulose nanocrystals for construction of hydrogel-based flexible glucose sensors for sweat detection
abstract: With the miniaturization, integration and intelligence of sweat electrochemical sensor technology, hydrogel flexible sensors have demonstrated immense potential in the field of real-time and non-invasive personal health monitoring.

title: The potential of carbohydrate supramolecular hydrogels for long-term 3D culture of primary fibroblasts
abstract: A pure small molecule, N -nonyl- d -galactonamide self-assembles into supramolecular fibers to give hydrogels. The cell growth and cell morphology, on the top and inside of these fibrous hydrogels, were analyzed over several weeks.

title: Multimodal sponge-based wearable sensor for continuous monitoring of electrochemical and electrophysiological signals during exercise
abstract: A conformal multimodal wearable patch with 3D sponge electrodes enables real-time simultaneous monitoring of sweat biomarkers (glucose/lactate) and electrophysiological signals (ECG/EMG) during exercise for personalized athletic health monitoring.

title: Rational design of near-infrared carbon dots as polarity-sensitive fluorescent probes for imaging of lipid droplets
abstract: This work summarized the luminescence mechanism and construction rules of NIR CDs. The synthesized NIR CDs could serve as promising smart fluorescence probes for sensing and imaging polarity changes in solutions and lipid droplets.

title: Thermoresponsive polymers for cell support: poloxamers as a case study of promise and challenge
abstract: Thermoresponsive P407 hydrogels offer rapid gelation and 3D printability, but face stability limits. We highlight protocol inconsistencies, emerging hybrid systems, and future directions for thermoresponsive biomaterials as culture cell culture supports.

title: Bioinspired hybrid DNA/dendrimer-based films with supramolecular chirality
abstract: Hybrid DNA/dendrimer films were obtained by heating dsDNA above its T m and, while in the denatured state, mixing it with dendrimers, followed by controlled cooling. Films are stable, cytocompatible and show evidence of supramolecular chirality.

title: Highly transparent and elastic acellular swim bladder with potential application in cornea implantation
abstract: A novel highly transparent and elastic decellularized swim bladder corneal scaffold was prepared by dehydration and cross-linking. Its optical properties, mechanical properties, stability and biocompatibility were characterized.

title: Computer-aided design of short peptide ligands targeting N -formyl peptide MT-ND6: potential application in treating severe inflammatory diseases
abstract: The peptide RF binds to MT-ND6 via multiple weak interactions, including hydrogen bonding, electrostatic interactions and nonpolar interactions. The binding affinity was confirmed by MD simulations and adsorption assay.

title: A near-infrared aggregation-induced emission photosensitizer with mitochondria specificity enhances radiotherapy for cancer stem cells ablation
abstract: A mitochondrial-targeting aggregation-induced emission photosensitizer was developed for tumor imaging and effectively kills cancer stem cells via enhanced radiotherapy by radiosensitizing effect.

title: A light-driven molecular motor-polypeptide conjugate supports controlled cell uptake
abstract: A light-driven molecular motor-polypeptide conjugate links a molecular motor to a mitochondria-targeted polyglutamate. Upon 405 nm light irradiation, it shifts from micelles to worm-like structures, boosting cellular uptake and specificity.

title: Treating rheumatoid arthritis using fibroblast-activated protein-α-responsive micelles through a cascade control program
abstract: LGPO/Dex micelles treat RA via a three-stage mechanism: (1) joint targeting, (2) inflammation-responsive drug release to normalize M1/M2 macrophage polarization, and (3) suppression of monocyte recruitment to prevent disease progression.

title: Strategic use of nanomaterials as double-edged therapeutics to control carcinogenesis via regulation of dysbiosis and bacterial infection: current status and future prospects
abstract: The present review focusses on an innovative therapeutic approach utilizing nanoparticle based strategies to simultaneously combat bacterial and cancer cell progression via targeting common cell surface receptors.

title: Low-cost, robust, and transportable devices based on Cu( i )–I cluster hybrid luminescent compounds as tetracycline sensors for contaminated waters
abstract: Stable suspensions of submicrometric particles based on the Cu( i )–I cluster, with high photoluminescence and band gap like tetracycline. Easily processed through immersion or 3D printing creating rapid-response, robust, and efficient, sensor devices.

title: Zwitterionic-based cyclic brush polymer nanomicelles with improved lubrication and antioxidation properties
abstract: Zwitterionic based cyclic brush polymers provide improved lubrication and antioxidant properties.

title: Bone-targeted carbon dots nanogels achieve precision antioxidant therapy for rheumatoid arthritis by activating the endogenous antioxidant system
abstract: Bone-targeted carbon dot nanogels (n(CDs)) enable localized reactive oxygen species scavenging via cascade catalysis, activate endogenous antioxidants, and alleviate rheumatoid arthritis by targeting bone tissues, outperforming free CDs.

title: Triggered by light and magnetism: smart foam PLLA/HAP/Fe 3 O 4 scaffolds for heat-controlled biomedical applications
abstract: Foams containing PLLA, HAP, and Fe 3 O 4 were obtained using TIPS technique with high responsiveness toward AMF and NIR stimulation.

title: Orlistat–dopamine conjugate micelles improve targeted delivery and therapeutic efficiency of camptothecin in combination chemotherapy
abstract: The development of orlistat–dopamine conjugated micelles as a nanocarrier for camptothecin to achieve synergistic combination chemotherapy with enhanced cancer cell targeting, uptake and two-stage cytotoxicity.

title: Polysiloxane-based polyurethane/quaternary ammonium-zwitterionic pressure-sensitive adhesive composite double dressing with antimicrobial and antifouling properties
abstract: Soft & robust protective layer based on controllable aggregation of polyurethane; combination of antimicrobial and antifouling based on cationic and zwitterionic ions; excellent adhesion and water vapor transmission rate for promoting wound healing.

title: Self-assembled peptide-based nanofibers for cardiovascular tissue regeneration
abstract: Self-assembled peptide nanofibers to enhance cardiac repair and regeneration.

title: Next-generation biopolymer gels: innovations in drug delivery and theranostics
abstract: Theranostic use of smart gels for precise treatment and drug delivery.

title: A CRISPR/Cas12a-based aptasensor enhanced by functionalized AuNPs for sensitive full-range C-reactive protein detection
abstract: A CRISPR/Cas12a-based aptasensor enabling full-range, ultrasensitive CRP detection with nanomaterial-enhanced fluorescence and clinical validation.

title: Angiogenesis–osteogenesis coupling and immunomodulatory CGRP@nano MOF-loaded CMCS/GelMA hydrogel for bone regeneration
abstract: A multifunctional platform (CGRP@MOF/CG hydrogel) integrating osteogenesis promotion, immunomodulation, angiogenesis enhancement, and antibacterial effects was developed to address the major challenges in the treatment of critical-sized bone defects.

title: Carbon dots meet artificial intelligence: applications in biomedical engineering
abstract: AI-driven advances in carbon dots (CDs) enable optimized synthesis, enhanced performance, and precise biomedical applications. This review highlights interdisciplinary progress and future directions at the interface of CDs and AI.

title: Impact of protein corona and light modulation on the antibacterial activity of light-activated silver nanoparticles
abstract: The critical role of environmental conditions on light-activated silver nanoparticles was elucidated. The multiple-irradiation approach of this platform maximizes the release of silver ions and promotes effective antibacterial action.

title: Hierarchically porous copper and gallium loaded sol–gel phosphate glasses for enhancement of wound closure
abstract: Hierarchically porous phosphate glass powders for wound healing. Significant wound healing promotion (up to 97%) was demonstrated using a human ex vivo wound model.

title: Ultrasound-responsive nanoweapons: covalent organic frameworks for cancer sonodynamic therapy
abstract: This review summarizes recent advancements in covalent organic framework (COF)-based cancer sonodynamic therapy, with an emphasis on the development of COF-based sonosensitizers and their significant roles in enhancing combination therapies.

title: Glucose microenvironment-primed nanocatalytic membranes for rapid bacterial eradication and infected diabetic wound regeneration
abstract: We developed a PLGA-based membrane (P-GA@GOx) with GaIn/Ag 2 S HJs and GOx to treat bacterial infections and aid tissue regeneration in diabetic wounds. It combines MIT/CDT/PTT/PDT for drug-free antibacterial action and glycemic control.

title: A MOF-derived iron oxide nanorod platform for multiplexed detection of ovarian cancer extracellular vesicle biomarkers
abstract: MOF-derived superparamagnetic iron oxide nanorods (MOF-IONRs) integrated with mesoporous Au-based SERS nanotags enable rapid, multiplexed EV biomarker detection of ovarian cancer, offering ultrasensitive performance with point-of-care applicability.

title: Machine learning-based label-free macrophage phenotyping in immune-material interactions
abstract: Label-free imaging and AI enable high-throughput, non-invasive macrophage phenotyping, uncovering immune responses to diverse collagen substrates.

title: Fabrication and characterization of a multifunctional alginate–gelatin–fibrinogen hydrogel for potential muscle tissue reconfiguration in vitro
abstract: In this study, we successfully fabricated alginate–gelatin–fibrinogen hydrogels with high performance stability, excellent printability, low swelling property, tunable mechanical properties, and ease of handling.

title: Emerging trends in wearable and non-invasive cortisol sensing technologies – a review
abstract: Emerging wearable cortisol sensors represent a significant innovation in personalized health monitoring, allowing for detection of cortisol, a key biomarker for stress, chronic diseases, and overall well-being.

title: Solid-phase engineering of molecularly imprinted nanoparticles (NanoMIPs): how template and solid-phase drive polymer composition and binding performance
abstract: The choice of the solid phase in the production of nanMIPs can play an important role in the final nanoMIP polymer composition.

title: Engineering curvature: block copolymer lithography for the fabrication of curved lipid membranes and their impact on protein–membrane interactions
abstract: We introduce a novel fabrication methodology using block-copolymer lithography to control local curvature of DOPC lipid membranes and demonstrate how this curvature affects CALM protein binding.

title: Photo-responsive antibacterial metal organic frameworks
abstract: Metal organic frameworks with diverse photo-responsive antibacterial mechanisms for biomedical applications are summarized.

title: Tumor-acidity triggered detachment of surface coatings and charge-reversal of fluorinated polymeric micelles to boost photodynamic cancer therapy
abstract: Photodynamic therapy efficiency is constrained by tumor hypoxia and insufficient photosensitizer accumulation. To address these limitations, an oxygen-supplying nanoplatform was developed through function-oriented polymer design.

title: 1,4-Bis(2-hydroxyethyl)piperazine-derived water-dispersible and antibacterial polyurethane coatings for medical catheters
abstract: Water-dispersible and bactericidal polyurethane coatings developed for biomedical applications.

title: Platelet-derived biomaterials for targeted drug delivery and tissue repair
abstract: Platelets are nucleic-free cells with a lifespan of 7–10 days in the bloodstream, playing a crucial role in various physiological processes such as hemostasis, thrombus formation, tumor development and metastasis, inflammation, and host defense.

title: Automated analysis of pore structures in biomaterials
abstract: Schematic representation of factors affecting automated pore size determination and requirements for improvement.

title: Hemoglobin-loaded ZIF-8 nanoparticles equipped with PEGylated metal-phenolic network coatings: an oxygen carrier with antioxidant and stealth properties
abstract: Hemoglobin-loaded ZIF-8 nanoparticles, coated with MPNs and PEG, demonstrated enhanced stability, biocompatibility, antioxidant properties, and efficient oxygen delivery, offering a promising alternative to traditional blood transfusions.

title: Digital light processing 3D printing of high-fidelity and versatile hydrogels via in situ phase separation
abstract: A DLP 3D printing strategy utilizing in situ phase separation is developed to fabricate high-fidelity hydrogels with ultra-high resolution, highly mechanical property, mechano-moisture sensitivity and biocompatibility.

title: A biocompatible, eco-friendly 3D-printed PCL-NIPUA resin for macular bucking devices in myopic tractional maculopathy
abstract: Pathological myopia, characterized by excessive axial elongation, posterior staphyloma (PS) and retinal-choroidal degeneration, underlies sight-threatening conditions such as myopic traction maculopathy (MTM) and macular atrophy.

title: Charge microenvironment and bioactivity of in situ -formed PEG-RGD dual hydrogel dressings promote wound healing
abstract: Schematic diagram of in situ formed polypeptide hydrogel synthesis and promotion of skin repair.

title: Recent developments in polymer chemistry, medicinal chemistry and electro-optics using Ni and Pd-based catalytic systems
abstract: Catalytic systems based on transition metal ions, especially palladium and nickel ions, have gained great interest, due to the variety of their structures, their ability to bind with other molecules and to stabilize active centers to form catalysts.

title: A nitric oxide-releasing organic selenium-grafted phosphorylcholine coating for thrombosis and inflammation prevention in extracorporeal membrane oxygenation
abstract: A dual-functional surface modification strategy was developed by first pre-functionalizing PMP hollow fiber membranes with selenocystamine, followed by grafting a phosphorylcholine copolymer MA(PCLA) to form a coating with combined anti-thrombotic and anti-inflammatory properties.

title: PEGylated W 4.6 N 4 /WO 3 nanoparticles as a near-infrared photothermal agent for effective cancer therapy
abstract: The heterostructured polyethylene glycol-modified W 4.6 N 4 /WO 3 nanoparticles are constructed. The nanoparticles show photothermal conversion under NIR irradiation and efficient tumor ablation function, which is potential for tumor photothermal therapy.

title: A 3D bioprinted in vitro full-thickness skin aging model
abstract: A 3D bioprinted skin aging model was developed using silk fibroin–gelatin bioink by co-culture senescent fibroblasts and keratinocytes. Oxidative stress by H 2 O 2 and high glucose were used as potential substitute agents for senescence induction.

title: Synthesis and characterization of poly(ester amide)-based materials for 3D printing of tissue engineering scaffolds
abstract: A new library of biocompatible AAA-PEAs with tunable physicochemical properties is proposed and validated for use as polymeric materials in the additive manufacturing (AM) of tissue engineering scaffolds.

title: Synergistic microwave hyperthermia treatment for subcutaneous deep in situ breast cancer using conformal array antennas and a microwave-thermal-sensitive nanomaterial
abstract: A microwave-thermal-sensitive ZIF-8@HA nanoplatform combined with conformal array antennas enhances MW thermal conversion efficiency, reduces tumor ablation time, and minimizes thermal damage to healthy tissues during treatment.

title: Enhanced yield and proteomic profile of osteoblast-derived extracellular vesicles from 3D MEW mPCL scaffolds
abstract: 3D melt electrowritten (MEW) polycaprolactone (mPCL) scaffolds enhance osteoblast-derived sEVs yield and enrich EV proteomes, highlighting proteins linked to cell adhesion, complement, and tight junctions, with potential for bone tissue engineering.

title: Advancements in 3D gel culture systems for enhanced angiogenesis in bone tissue engineering
abstract: Various processing technologies for a 3D gel culture system used in bone tissue angiogenesis: bioprinting, organ decellularization, microfluidics, photolithography and electrospinning.

title: Correction: The design and synthesis of redox-responsive oridonin polymeric prodrug micelle formulation for effective gastric cancer therapy
abstract: Correction for ‘The design and synthesis of redox-responsive oridonin polymeric prodrug micelle formulation for effective gastric cancer therapy’ by Luzhou Xu et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2021, 9 , 3068–3078, https://doi.org/10.1039/D1TB00127B.

title: Benchmarking of pH-responsive mixed micelles for repurposed breast cancer therapy of ibrutinib with molecular modeling and pharmacokinetic insights
abstract: Developed pH-sensitive polymeric mixed micelles to repurpose ibrutinib for breast cancer. Formulation enabled burst release at pH 6.5, improved cytotoxicity in MCF-7 and MDA-MB-231 cells, and addressed IBT’s low bioavailability and safety issues.

title: Tuning the structure of thienoisoindigo (TIG) copolymers to afford bright near-infrared emission for bioimaging through aggregation-enhanced emission
abstract: Structural tuning of thienoisoindigo copolymers enables aggregation-enhanced emission and record-high near-infrared brightness yielding scalable, biocompatible nanoparticle emitters optimized for in vivo imaging.

title: Comprehensive insights into electrochemical nicotine sensing technologies
abstract: Electrochemical sensing of nicotine using nanostructured materials modified electrodes.

title: A T 1 MRI detectable hyaluronic acid hydrogel for in vivo tracking after intracerebral injection in stroke
abstract: A T 1 MRI detectable hyaluronic acid hydrogel that is injectable and self-healing was developed for in vivo tracking in stroke conditions. The labeling strategy enabled monitoring its distribution and degradation through T 1 measurements.

title: High-performance pressure sensors based on MXene@CNT coaxial nanofiber-coated yarns for multipoint motion detection and health monitoring
abstract: Coaxial nanofiber-coated yarns through electrospinning and MXene@CNTs are prepared. The PDMS–MCTPU/AY pressure sensor exhibits excellent sensing performance. The sensing array effectively identifies the location and magnitude of pressure.

title: Research trends and advanced applications of gelatin in cartilage tissue engineering: a combined bibliometric and comprehensive review
abstract: This bibliometric review of 1276 publications outlines the evolution of gelatin in cartilage engineering, highlighting advances in smart hydrogels, stem cell control, spatiotemporal delivery, and 3D/4D bioprinting for dynamic regeneration.

title: Enhanced catalytic efficiency of nanozymes with a V-structured chip for microfluidic biosensing of S. typhimurium
abstract: By enhancing the catalytic efficiency of the Fe 3 O 4 /Au nanozyme using a corresponding V-structure in the microfluidic chip, a colorimetric method was built on an integrated microfluidic platform for sensitive biosensing of Salmonella typhimurium .

title: Injectable self-healing hydrogels loaded with Crinis Carbonisatus nanoparticles for rapid hemostasis and wound healing
abstract: Catechol-modified hydroxypropyl chitosan/oxidized dextran-based hydrogels loaded with Crinis Carbonisatus nanoparticles exhibit injectability, adhesion, and antibacterial and antioxidant properties for rapid hemostasis and wound healing.

title: Photocrosslinkable starch cinnamyl ethers as bioinspired bio-based polymers
abstract: Starch-cinnamyl ethers are synthesized, shaped into thin films and characterized for their ability to undergo photo crosslinking.

title: Nanoparticles modified with glucose analogs to enhance the permeability of the blood–brain barrier and their accumulation in the epileptic brain
abstract: Nanoparticles were modified with glucose analogs to facilitate BBB penetration and on-demand brain accumulation of nanoparticles was achieved in the epileptic brain.

title: A spider silk-inspired, transparent, anti-freezing ionic conductive hydrogel as a flexible sensor device
abstract: The CPQA–EH hydrogel has remarkable optical transparency, anti-freezing and antibacterial properties, and exhibits excellent mechanical properties. Sensors based on the CPQA–EH hydrogel exhibited quick response to strain/pressure external stimuli.

title: A zwitterionic dual-network hydrogel patch with anti-inflammatory properties for efficient peritoneal adhesion prevention
abstract: A zwitterionic dual-network hydrogel patch (GPSB), combining a gelatin-metal polyphenol network and a polysulfobetaine network, shows strong antifouling, antibacterial, and healing effects, effectively preventing peritoneal adhesion post-surgery.

title: Antibacterial abilities of Vancomycin interactions in PDEGMA brushes fabricated by interface-mediated PET-RAFT polymerization on implant surfaces
abstract: We reported a bacteria-surface interactions of Vancomycin in different states (release and covalent binding) were investigated on PDEGMA brushes with temperature-sensitive carboxylic acid functional end groups.

title: Experimental and computational techniques to investigate the protein resistance of zwitterionic polymers
abstract: Zwitterionic polymers exhibit exceptional protein resistance due to their hydration layer. Both experimental and computational techniques offer insights into the hydration effect and underlying mechanisms.

title: Correction: Construction and evaluation of platelet concentrate/mesoporous bioactive glass composite scaffolds for bone repair: injectable platelet-rich fibrin (i-PRF) vs. concentrated growth factors (CGF)
abstract: Correction for ‘Construction and evaluation of platelet concentrate/mesoporous bioactive glass composite scaffolds for bone repair: injectable platelet-rich fibrin (i-PRF) vs. concentrated growth factors (CGF)’ by Yuanyuan Guo et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2025, https://doi.org/10.1039/D5TB00413F.

title: Luminescent ultrashort peptide hydrogelator with enhanced photophysical implications and biocompatibility
abstract: A novel ultra-short peptide hydrogelator capable of exhibiting excellent aggregation-induced emission and biocompatible features that have been exploited for in vitro cellular imaging.

title: Ultra-fast and multi-responsive anisotropic nanofibrous actuator with remote control
abstract: A soft actuator enabling facile integration of multi-stimuli responsiveness. A universal strategy for designing soft actuators with multi-responsiveness, programmable deformation, and ultrafast actuation.

title: Geometrically constrained cytoskeletal reorganisation modulates DNA nanostructures uptake
abstract: DNA nanostructures (DNs) are promising for biomedical applications due to their programmability, easy synthesis, functionalization, and low toxicity. This study reveals how constrained cytoskeletal remodelling influences cellular uptake of DNs.

title: Enhancing the mechanical and antibacterial properties of hydroxyapatite bioceramics by in situ graphene doping to promote osseointegration in infected bone defects
abstract: HA ceramics with in situ graphene doping were fabricated by the ball milling and vacuum sintering in a convenient way, which not only enhanced their flexural strength and fracture toughness, but also imparted efficient antibacterial properties to promote osseointegration in infected bone defect.

title: Inducing ferroptosis in breast cancer with nanoparticles: a promising and challenging therapeutic strategy
