abstract: pH-responsive injectable nanocomposite hydrogel loading Pt–TiO 2 nanoparticles and fluorescence probe can achieve synergistic sonodynamic-immunotherapy and imaging of breast cancer.

title: Crafting nature's wonders: nanoarchitectonics developments in bioinspired nanocellulose-based stimuli-responsive supramolecular matrices
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive overview of the recent advances in the development of stimuli-responsive nanocellulose-based drug delivery systems.

title: Nanovehicles for delivery of antigens and adjuvants as cancer nanovaccines
abstract: This review summarizes cancer nanovaccine delivery via inorganic nanoparticles, polymeric nanovehicles, nanoengineered coordination polymers, lipid nanovehicles, biomimetic nanovehicles, virus-like particles, and self-assembled peptide vehicles.

title: Heterogeneous piezo-self-Fenton material design: an intersecting solution for pollutant degradation and tumor therapy
abstract: The commonalities and differences of EPSF catalysts in organic pollutant degradation and tumor-targeted therapy are summarized, focusing on catalytic mechanisms, material systems, performance enhancement strategies, and catalyst design.

title: Colorimetric pH-responsive nanofibrous hydrogels for in vitro monitoring of wound infection
abstract: Effective wound management is crucial for improving patient outcomes, particularly through timely detection of infections and monitoring of wound conditions.

title: Engineered hollow Cu 2 O@ZnO p–n heterojunction nanocomposites for synergistic photocatalytic disinfection and tissue regeneration
abstract: Hollow porous Cu 2 O@ZnO was synthesized via a simple hydrothermal method. It exhibits enhanced visible-light response and high-efficiency photocatalytic antibacterial activity, promising for infected wound healing.

title: Recent advances in structural and functional design of electrospun nanofibers for wound healing
abstract: A schematic overview of electrospinning technology and the structure of electrospun nanofibers.

title: Mechanistic understanding of pH as a driving force in cancer therapeutics
abstract: The development of pH-directed nanoparticles for tumor targeting represents a significant advancement in cancer biology and therapeutic strategies.

title: An oxidized dextran and thiolated chitosan-based hydrogel driven biomimetic triple negative breast cancer 3D in vitro model for cancer progression and therapeutic studies
abstract: This study presents a modified dextran–chitosan (MDC) hydrogel as a promising 3D substrate for in vitro 3D modelling TNBC, recapitulating key tumor-promoting factors for translational cancer research and precision drug screening.

title: Innovative diopside–MnFe 2 O 4 nanocomposites: a multifunctional platform for bone regeneration and hyperthermia therapy featuring MnFe 2 O 4 nanoparticles with near-bulk magnetic performance
abstract: Diopside–MnFe 2 O 4 nanocomposites, integrating MnFe 2 O 4 nanoparticles with near-bulk magnetization, enable bone repair, magnetothermal therapy (41–46 °C), and osteogenesis, offering a multifunctional platform for orthopedic and oncological applications.

title: Fabrication of cell culture devices with integrated microfluidic networks via vat multimaterial 3D printing
abstract: In this study a 3D printed wellplate for the dynamic culture of in vitro models has been developed, with a design that can enable the testing using conventional instrumentation for cell culture analysis and monitoring.

title: Pluronic nanoparticle-modified modular bacterial robots for therapy of tumors and inflammatory bowel disease
abstract: PF127-CHO nanomicelles anchored on bacterial surfaces enable stable drug loading and delivery, creating a versatile bacterial robot effective in cancer and colitis therapy with broad applicability and translational potential.

title: E-seed skin: a carbohydrate–protein hybrid nanostructure for delayed germination and accelerated growth
abstract: A carbohydrate–protein hybrid nanocoating on the tomato seed acts as a shield cum nutrient reservoir and improves seed viability. Such multifunctional coatings with humidity and microbial resistance can pave the way for smart coating technologies.

title: Engineering a biocompatible chitosan/MOF aerogel for hyperbilirubinemia treatment in liver failure
abstract: A biocompatible HKUST-1/chitosan aerogel enables efficient bilirubin removal and antibacterial action for blood purification in liver failure.

title: 3D printable collagen-like protein hydrogels via dynamic covalent assembly for soft tissue engineering
abstract: This study presents a novel 3D collagen-like protein/aldehyde-functionalized dextran (CLP–AD) hydrogel designed for tissue engineering.

title: Dual-functional PCN-242 (Fe 2 Co) MOF for sensitive bacterial endotoxin detection
abstract: This work introduces a novel bimetallic MOF with superior endotoxin detection and enhanced catalytic activity for broader diagnostic applications.

title: Capturing the sol and gel states of thermoresponsive poly(2-oxazoline)/-(2-oxazine) hydrogels by ambient and subambient solid-state NMR
abstract: Two variants of pMeOx- b -pPheOzi- b -pMeOx with methyl or methoxy substituents were synthesized and used to test different NMR techniques for their characterization. Sample freezing provided new insights into the structure and different types of water.

title: Temporal and spatial control over fiber alignment within hyaluronic acid hydrogels using magnetic fields
abstract: Magnetically-responsive fibers embedded within hydrogels enable spatial and temporal control over fiber alignment via the application of a magnetic field. These materials could mimic the complex spatiotemporal organization of fibers within tissues.

title: A self-adaptive adhesive, mechanically enhanced, and antibacterial non-woven fabric wound dressing functionalized by a semi-interpenetrating network hydrogel for promoting infected wound healing
abstract: A self-adaptive adhesive wound dressing was developed by decorating NF with a semi-interpenetrating hydrogel via coating–heat curing, exhibiting satisfied mechanical, adhesion, and antibacterial properties, which are conformable for infected wounds.

title: Bipyramidal gold nanoparticles-assisted plasmonic photothermal therapy for ocular applications
abstract: The potential use of plasmonic photothermal therapy mediated by bipyramidal gold nanoparticles in ophthalmology has been described for the first time.

title: Responsive hydrogel modulator with self-regulated polyphenol release for accelerating diabetic wound healing via precise immunoregulation
abstract: Responsive hydrogels were capable of achieving the spatiotemporally controlled release of polyphenol that relieved severe oxidative stress and modulated macrophage polarization, thus accelerating diabetic wound healing.

title: Programmable spherical nucleic acids integrated with MOF-confined copper nanoclusters facilitate electrochemiluminescence detection of prostate-specific biomarkers
abstract: The designed all-in-one SNAs anchored with ice-cream probes achieve three functions: (i) enhanced AMACR conversion efficiency; (ii) promoted rolling circle amplification efficiency; and (iii) improved CRISPR/Cas12a activation efficiency.

title: Decellularized small intestinal hydrogel-encapsulated BMSCs attenuate dextran sulfate sodium-induced colitis in mice via immunomodulation and tissue repair
abstract: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) requires new therapies. Mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) grafts show poor survival. Decellularized small intestinal (dSI) hydrogel aids repair but lacks immunomodulation. Combining both may overcome these limits.

title: Broad-spectrum antimicrobial effects of hydrogen boride nanosheets
abstract: Thin films of hydrogen boride (HB) nanosheets exhibit high antimicrobial activity against bacteria, fungi, and viruses including SARS-CoV-2.

title: Colloidal gold immunochromatographic strips for the detection of kynurenine in clinical samples
abstract: A specific monoclonal antibody against KYN has been fabricated to develop a colloidal gold immunochromatographic assay platform for the qualitative and quantitative analysis of KYN in clinical human plasma samples, as confirmed by LC-MS/MS.

title: Antibacterial carbon dots integrating multiple mechanisms for selective Gram-positive bacteria elimination and infected wound healing acceleration
abstract: Schematic diagrams of (a) PR-CDs for selectively killing Gram-positive bacteria based on multiple antibacterial mechanisms and (b) PR-CDs@GG hydrogels with antibacterial properties for wound healing.

title: Light-activated antimicrobial polymers with citronellol-enhanced bacterial accumulation for on-demand disinfection
abstract: Synergistic photobactericidal polymers that integrate hydrophilic toluidine blue O with lipophilic citronellol exhibit significantly improved photodynamic antimicrobial activity against a variety of pathogens, including multidrug-resistant bacteria.

title: Zwitterionic polymer-coated magnetic nanoparticle induced chemotherapy and ferroptosis for triple-negative breast cancer therapy
abstract: Zwitterionic polymer PSBMA-coated gambogenic acid (GNA)-loaded magnetic composite nanoparticles (Fe 3 O 4 @PSBMA-GNA) were fabricated for chemotherapy combined with ferroptosis for triple-negative breast cancer therapy.

title: Exploring PEKK: properties, applications and promise of personalized medicine
abstract: Overview of PEKK's chemical, physical properties and 3D printing suitability. Evaluation of the potential of PEKK-based personalized medicine.

title: From fundamentals to applications: magnetic nanoparticles for MRI imaging and NIR-induced thermal activation in tissue-mimicking environments
abstract: Magnetic nanoparticles generate heat under NIR light and act as MRI contrast agents when exposed to a magnetic field. Their dual behavior is evaluated in tissue-mimicking phantoms to bridge the gap between solution studies and clinical relevance.

title: Dynamic multistage hydrogel with auto-adjusting networks for sequential anti-infection, anti-inflammation, and angiogenesis in infected wound regeneration
abstract: A near-infrared (NIR)-responsive and versatile therapeutic hydrogel is fabricated. The prepared hydrogel displays antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and angiogenic properties, reaching a long-lasting, safe therapeutic effect on wound healing.

title: Selective fluorescence detection of proteins using molecularly imprinted hydrogels with aggregation-induced emission
abstract: We newly fabricated molecularly imprinted hydrogels (MIHs) with a fluorescent monomer. The MIHs showed a remarkable increase in fluorescence upon adsorption of the target protein, demonstrating high selectivity compared to other proteins.

title: Graphene-based neural electrodes: from materials and device fabrication to properties
abstract: This review explores the core research directions and their underlying rationales in graphene-based neural electrode studies.

title: Predicting inflammatory response of biomimetic nanofibre scaffolds for tissue regeneration using machine learning and graph theory
abstract: Assessing macrophage behavior on scaffolds is vital for successful tissue engineering. CNNs could classify macrophage phenotypes and graph theory revealed that nanofiber scaffolds mimic extracellular matrix connectivity, improving healing outcomes.

title: Multi-technique computational assessment of fluoride uptake in enamel using PIGE, NEXAFS, and Raman spectroscopy
abstract: This study explores F incorporation in enamel via PIGE, NEXAFS, and Raman spectroscopy, integrating experimental/simulated spectra. Machine Learning boosts data interpretation, offering key insights into F delivery for clinical application.

title: Effective repair of endometrial injury in rats using enzyme cross-linked gelatin hydrogels with human menstrual blood-derived stem cells
abstract: Hydrogel delivery of mesenchymal stem cell spheroids repairs injured uterus and restores fertility in rats.

title: Mortise-tenon inspired silk fibroin–tannic acid/platelet-rich fibrin composite hydrogel enhances bone regeneration
abstract: A mortise-tenon inspired ST-iPRF composite hydrogel achieves structural-biological synergy through complementary design: ST hydrogel provides mechanical support while iPRF delivers growth factors, enabling enhanced bone regeneration.

title: Yeast biofilm synapse: an intra-kingdom pathway to high-density current output in bioelectronic devices
abstract: Encapsulating S. cerevisiae in a hydrogel enhances biofilm organization and boosts bioelectricity generation.

title: Polyurethane packed graphene-coated spider silk by dip-casting for a highly stretchable strain sensor
abstract: In recent years, naturally occurring materials have gained tremendous attention for their potential in the fabrication of advanced wearable electronic devices.

title: Synthesis of π-extended CinNapht fluorophores via late-stage Pd-catalyzed C–N and C–C bond formation and application for selective imaging of lipid droplets in living cells
abstract: We report the efficient synthesis of a 4′-brominated CinNapht scaffold, which enables the straightforward preparation of multiple new dyes through late-stage functionalization via palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling reactions.

title: A superhydrophilic PEGDA-coated PBT membrane prepared by photoinitiated polymerization for hemodialysis therapy
abstract: A poly(ethylene glycol) diacrylate coating was fabricated on the surface of hydrophobic poly(butylene terephthalate)-based dialysis membranes. This coating imparts superhydrophilicity, significantly enhancing its hemodialysis performance.

title: Injectable biocompatible hydrogels with tunable strength based on crosslinked supramolecular polymer nanofibers
abstract: Shear-thinning biocompatible hydrogels are created through supramolecular fiber formation of amphiphilic polymers with hydrogen-bonding motifs based on peptide units. Chemical variations allow tuning of the rheological properties over a wide range.

title: Guiding cellular behavior with micro/nanostructured cellulose acetate substrates fabricated using femtosecond laser lithography
abstract: A schematic showing fs laser-assisted fabrication of micro/nanostructured patterns on cellulose acetate, which were used to guide muscle cell growth, resulting in aligned cells with elongated nuclei, enhanced actin expression and coherency.

title: Iron-doped carbon dots with multi-enzyme activities for rapid determination of phenolic compounds
abstract: Iron-doped carbon dots (FeCDs) exhibit peroxidase-like, catalase-like, and photo-enhanced laccase-like activities, and were successfully applied for the rapid determination of phenolic compounds.

title: Traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) enhances the therapeutic efficiency of a gemcitabine-loaded injectable hydrogel on postoperative breast cancer through modulating the microenvironment
abstract: GEM-hydrogel was prepared for local treatment of postoperative breast cancer. Meanwhile, SBP was given to normalize the blood vessels for enhancing drug perfusion by promoting PDGFB expression, pericytes recruiting and alleviating hypoxia.

title: Dual-color reversible fluorescent carbon dots designed for dynamic monitoring of cellular superoxide anion radicals
abstract: Synthesis of dual-color fluorescent CDs and schematic of their dynamic reversible detection of O 2 ˙ − .

title: Advancements in anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction based on weaving technology: current developments and future prospects
abstract: Weaving-inspired scaffolds offer enhanced mechanical strength and cellular interactions for anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction, demonstrating promising potential for clinical translation.

title: Monomer exchange dynamics in ureido–pyrimidinone supramolecular polymers via molecular simulations
abstract: A synergistic integration of molecular dynamics, enhanced sampling, and data analysis has elucidated, at atomistic resolution, the key factors governing monomer exchange dynamics in synthetic ureido-pyrimidone supramolecular polymers.

title: Polycyclic aromatic polymer nanoparticles show potent infectious particle adsorption capability
abstract: Newly synthesised polycyclic aromatic polymer nanoparticles with different structures showed high adsorption capacity for SARS-CoV-2 infectious particles, but the adsorption capacity was found to depend on the polymer structure.

title: Near infrared light driven nanocatalyst with hole-mediated GSH-depletion for augmented memory therapy
abstract: The 808 nm light-driven nanocatalysts are widely used in the field of enhanced memory therapy through the generation of reactive oxygen species and depletion of glutathione.

title: Shark skin-inspired PDA-coated and peptide-functionalized wrinkled chitosan/gelatin microspheres for hemostasis and infected wound healing
abstract: Chitosan-based microspheres loaded with a thrombin agonist peptide, inspired by the unique hydrodynamic ridge structure of shark skin, have been developed for efficient hemostasis, antibacterial activity, and wound healing promotion.

title: Iron( ii )-catalysed tyrosinase crosslinked hyaluronic acid hydrogel for the controlled release of human antibodies
abstract: Tyrosinase is a common crosslinker used in the formation of in situ hydrogels, often resulting in significantly longer gelation times.

title: Bioinspired silk protein modification to develop instant dissolvable microneedles with superior mechanical properties and long-term biomolecule stabilization
abstract: Biomimetically modified silk (MS) used to fabricate MS-DMN exhibits instant dissolvability, high mechanical strength, and improved biomolecule stability at room temperature, enabling cold chain-free storage for various biomedical applications.

title: Advances in hydrogel systems for bone regeneration: trends, innovations, and prospects
abstract: Research hotspots and design strategies for bone regeneration hydrogels.

title: An electrically conductive gellan gum/polyvinyl alcohol interpenetrating network hydrogel: a dual crosslinked 3D printing ink for cardiac tissue
abstract: Dual crosslinkable hydrogels enables incorporation of electro-conductive materials and cells for 3D biofabrication of cardiac tissue constructs.

title: A functional 2D MXene–DNA hybrid hydrogel for portable detection of blood disorder biomarker thrombin in human plasma
abstract: A schematic representation of the workflow of the thrombin detection system. The addition of a sample containing thrombin to the MXene–DNA hydrogel leads to specific resistance after applying it to the Pt-printed button circuit.

title: Insights into advanced dressings based on shape memory materials for wound management
abstract: The figure shows shape memory materials enabling dynamic wound adaptation via light, water, or heat. They promote hemostasis, contraction, and controlled drug release, offering smart, responsive dressings for precise, efficient wound healing.

title: Eco-friendly self-cleaning coatings: fundamentals, fabrication, applications, and sustainability
abstract: Eco-friendly self-cleaning coatings have garnered significant attention due to their potential to address environmental concerns while offering remarkable properties.

title: Engineering long-term controlled drug release from biodegradable devices 3D printed with vat polymerization
abstract: This study shows that long-term controlled release of small molecule drugs could be achieved by modifying the architecture of 3D-printed devices made with biodegradable polymers.

title: A synthetic biomolecular condensate from plant proteins with controlled colloidal properties
abstract: A synthetic biomolecular condensate (sBC) was developed using zein, a hydrophobic protein derived from plants. These particles can be used as an artifiical platform to understand the structure and function of natural protein-rich condensates.

title: A virus-inspired RNA mimicry approach for effective cancer immunotherapy
abstract: Novel cancer therapy using uridine-rich nanoparticles (UNPs) to mimic viral RNA, activating RIG-I-like receptors in cancer cells to enhance cancer immunotherapy.

title: Anti-swelling, antithrombotic and antibacterial zwitterionic hydrogel coatings with a sandwich structure on polymer substrates
abstract: A sandwich-structured zwitterionic hydrogel coating with anti-swelling, antithrombotic, and antibacterial properties was developed for medical catheters to enhance clinical safety and efficacy.

title: Unraveling the synergistic effects of Ag, Li and Sr on Zn alloys in enhancing orthopedic repair potential
abstract: Recently, Li, which can greatly enhance the mechanical characteristics of zinc alloys, Ag, which has antibacterial properties, and Sr, which promotes bone formation, have been widely applied in biodegradable alloys.

title: Click chemistry-driven adhesive hydrogel for efficient healing of infected wounds through multistage comprehensive management
abstract: Click chemistry-driven hydrogel: multistage strategy for efficient healing of infected wounds.

title: The effect of Si/Al ratio and drug loading level on the molecular behaviour and controlled release of 5-fluorouracil from zeolite H-Beta
abstract: Anticancer drug release from Beta zeolites of varying composition showed slower release from samples with fewer adsorption sites. Neutron spectroscopy and molecular simulations help attribute this to increased drug–drug interactions in the pores.

title: Synergistic chemo-photothermal therapy and osteogenic activity using graphene oxide-functionalized composite whitlockite bone particles
abstract: Bioactive WG@DOX microparticles integrate NIR-triggered drug delivery and photothermal therapy with osteoinductive properties for synergistic osteosarcoma treatment and bone regeneration.

title: A platinum( iv )–crocetin nanoplatform with intracellular morphological transformation for enhanced colorectal cancer therapy
abstract: Pt( iv )–Cro NPs demonstrate concentration-dependent self-assembly and intracellular transformation, enhancing cisplatin's antitumor efficacy against colorectal cancer through boosted cytotoxicity, mitochondrial damage, and ROS generation.

title: Making the negative positive – fluorination of indole as an efficient strategy to improve guanidinium-containing gene carriers
abstract: Fluorination unlocks optimal gene delivery: precise tuning of polymer hydrophobicity boosts DNA release and transfection—showing that more isn't always better.

title: Design of a nonlinear metastructure for temperature detection and biosensing based on the second harmonic generation in theory
abstract: A topological diagram of a NM layered structure. Dielectrics filled with different colors represent the overall structure.

title: A multifunctional metal-based nanozyme for CT/PTI-guided photothermal/starvation/chemodynamic therapy against colon tumor
abstract: A metal-based nanozyme CeAIP was synthesized through a simple method. It integrates PTT, CDT, ST, and intrinsic catalytic activity. CeAIP also enables CT and photothermal imaging for efficient diagnosis and therapy.

title: A piezoelectric hydrogel containing bismuth sulfide with cationic vacancies with enhanced sonodynamic/nanozyme activity for synergistically killing bacteria and boosting osteoblast differentiation
abstract: A piezoelectric hydrogel containing bismuth sulfide with cationic vacancies was developed, using ultrasound-triggered piezoelectricity to boost sonodynamic effects and nanozyme activities for killing bacteria and promoting osteoblast differentiation.

title: Bioinspired osteoblast-imprinted piezoelectric PDMS/BaTiO 3 nanocomposites accelerate the osteogenic differentiation of adipose-derived stem cells under mechanical stimulation
abstract: The bioinspired cell culture substrate regulates the adipose-derived stem cells' functions and fate by exploiting the synergistic effect of osteoblast-imprinting and mechanoelectrical stimulation.

title: Hydrolytic degradation of PEG-based thiol–norbornene hydrogels enables multi-modal controlled release
abstract: Poly(ethylene glycol)-norbornene (PEGNB) and PEGNB-carboxylate (PEGNB CA ) are photocrosslinked into hydrogels with highly tunable hydrolytic degradation to support multi-modal delivery of proteins with different sizes.

title: Phosphorothioated DNA engineered fusion liposomes for ultrasound-responsive targeted intracellular protein delivery
abstract: Ultrasound-triggered cell-targeted protein delivery is achieved by pre-designed fusion liposomes, a system consisting of protein-loaded liposomes and an ultrasound-responsive fusion unit.

title: An injectable carboxymethyl chitosan–polyaniline/oxidized dextran triple-network hydrogel for long-term gastrointestinal tissue marking and photothermal tumor therapy
abstract: A biocompatible chitosan–polyaniline/oxidized dextran (CMCS–PANI/OD) hydrogel provides durable gastrointestinal tissue marking and enables photothermal tumor ablation under near-infrared irradiation.

title: An oxidized chondroitin sulfate-crosslinked and CuCDs-loaded decellularized bovine pericardium with improved anti-coagulation, pro-endothelialization and anti-calcification properties for BHVs
abstract: The multifunctionality of CuCDs–OCS–BP with anti-coagulation, anti-calcification and anti-inflammatory properties and a strong ability to promote endothelial proliferation is presented.

title: Application and prospects of polysaccharide-based hydrogels in the treatment of intervertebral disc degeneration
abstract: Intervertebral disc degeneration (IVDD) is a primary contributor to chronic low back pain (LBP) and neurological dysfunction. Polysaccharide-based hydrogels hold tremendous potential for the treatment of IVDD.

title: Copper oxide nanoparticles as delivery vehicles for different Pt( ii )-drugs: experimental and theoretical evaluation
abstract: Schematic representation of adsorption and release of cisplatin from CuO NPS.

title: Multifunctional metal–phenolic nanoparticles with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects for osteomyelitis management
abstract: Zn 2+ –phenolic nanoparticles are prepared by the self-assembly of Zn 2+ , EPL, and QU. This innovative strategy for the simultaneous delivery of transition metals ions and polyphenols may open up a new way to effectively treat osteomyelitis in vivo .

title: Advances in cuproptosis harnessing copper-based nanomaterials for cancer therapy
abstract: This review discusses the recent developments in copper-based nanomaterials that utilize copper-induced cell death, categorized by materials systems, while highlighting the limitations of current cuproptosis related copper-based nanomaterials.

title: Expression of concern: Plasmonic photothermal destruction of uropathogenic E. coli with reduced graphene oxide and core/shell nanocomposites of gold nanorods/reduced graphene oxide
abstract: Expression of concern for ‘Plasmonic photothermal destruction of uropathogenic E. coli with reduced graphene oxide and core/shell nanocomposites of gold nanorods/reduced graphene oxide’ by Kostiantyn Turcheniuk et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2015, 3 , 375–386, https://doi.org/10.1039/C4TB01760A.

title: Co-delivery of icariside II and doxorubicin by self-assembled carrier-free nanofibers for anti-lung cancer therapy
abstract: A novel carrier-free nanofiber system self-assembles from icariside II and doxorubicin via hydrogen bonding, enabling controlled release, enhanced uptake, and superior anti-lung cancer efficacy compared to single-drug therapies.

title: Staying one step ahead of chronic wounds by designing symbiotic, responsive functionality into dynamic nanohydrogels
abstract: Nanomaterial functionalised hydrogels enable dynamic, symbiotic wound dressings that provide adaptive, sustained healing for chronic wounds by integrating multifunctional responsive materials across multiple length scales.

title: Ligand-free C–H functionalization using a palladium nanocatalyst for the synthesis of 2-phenylindoles and their anticancer potential
abstract: A sustainable, magnetically recoverable Pd@FO-BCN nanocatalyst with minimal Pd loading is presented, enabling efficient ligand-free C–H functionalization for the synthesis of 2-phenylindole.

title: Bacteria-visualizing nano-bactericide with anti-inflammation and wound healing properties for in situ NIR phototherapeutic strategies
abstract: A phototherapeutic bactericide was constructed via covalently grafting silicon phthalocyanine onto chitosan, which superimpose the photodynamic–photothermal combined therapy with wound healing for anti-inflammation and wound recovery.

title: Enhanced luminescence and stability of TFMDSA nanoparticles via polymer-induced aggregation for bioimaging
abstract: We present the synthesis of two TFMDSA nanoparticles, demonstrating outstanding luminescence efficiency and stability. These nanoparticles were subsequently employed in cellular imaging applications, yielding satisfactory imaging outcomes.

title: Nebulized inhalation drug delivery: clinical applications and advancements in research
abstract: Nebulized inhalation is a non-invasive drug delivery method with fast onset, low dosage, and fewer side effects. This review covers its principles, devices, clinical applications, and future trends in respiratory and systemic diseases.

title: Lyophilized tumour cell-loaded 10 B-doped carbon dots for simultaneous boron neutron capture therapy and enhancement of antitumor immunity of prostate cancer
abstract: L-RM1@ 10 B-CD mediated boron neutron capture therapy induces immune state transition, DNA damage, and ferroptosis in prostate cancer tissue.

title: Advances in hydrogels combined with photothermal/photodynamic therapy for bacterial infection
abstract: This review summarizes advances in photothermal/photodynamic antimicrobial hydrogels, including design strategies, antibacterial mechanisms, and biomedical applications.

title: Bioactive zeolitic imidazolate framework nanoconjugates as synergistic drug delivery agents for cancer nanotherapeutics
abstract: Bioactive ZIF-67 and ZIF-8 nanocarriers functionalized with saponin and apigenin demonstrate synergistic drug delivery and apoptosis induction, offering potential for targeted cancer nanotherapeutics with minimal side effects.

title: Microneedle-mediated exosome delivery: a precision strategy in advanced regenerative medicine
abstract: The microneedle-mediated delivery of exosomes has diverse applications in regenerative medicine, encompassing the repair of skin, nerve, cartilage, oral, and cardiovascular tissues.

title: Impact of inner hydrophobicity of dendrimer nanomicelles on biodistribution: a PET imaging study
abstract: This study reports the impact of increased hydrophobicity of the amphiphilic dendrimer on hydrophilic/hydrophobic balance and consequently on the self-assembly and subsequent biodistribution.

title: Injectable biodegradable polysaccharide-based hydrogels for stem cell delivery and cartilage regeneration
abstract: An injectable and biodegradable polysaccharide hydrogel supports hADSC viability and chondrogenic differentiation, showing promise for cartilage regeneration and cell-based therapies.

title: Nitrogen doped carbon dots for in vitro intracellular redox modulation via optical stimulation
abstract: Carbon dots (CDs) are promising candidates as oxygen photosensitizers, in cancer therapeutic applications due to their high quantum yield, superior chemical and photostability, low cytotoxicity and ease of chemical functionalization/tuning.

title: Dual-functional guanosine-based hydrogel: high-efficiency protection in radiation-induced oral mucositis
abstract: This dual-functional supramolecular hydrogel represents a promising clinical strategy for preventing radiation-induced oral mucositis (RIOM).

title: Biomaterials enhancing localized cancer therapy activated anti-tumor immunity: a review
abstract: This article reviews the application of biomaterials in combination with immunotherapy to enhance the localized treatment of tumors, along with the current challenges and future development directions in this field.

title: One-pot synthesis of enhanced dye-sensitized persistent luminescence nanoparticles to alleviate concentration quenching
abstract: Through a one-pot method, dye-sensitized PLNPs (ZGC 1.2 @OAm–RhB) with a higher Cr 3+ doping (1.2%) were prepared. Enhanced energy transfer processes led to a 60-fold intensity increase and alleviated concentration quenching.

title: Tailored surface topographical scaffolds: a breakthrough in osteoarthritic cartilage and subchondral bone defect repair
abstract: This review highlights tailored surface topographies in scaffolds for osteoarthritis therapy, summarizing design principles, fabrication methods, and micro/nano topography effects on cell behavior via signaling pathways, guiding future therapies.

title: Room-temp trace ammonia detection using SnS 2 nanosheets@PANI composite: a step towards ultrasensitive environment monitoring and non-invasive renal disease diagnosis
abstract: Heterojunction SnS 2 /PANI nanocomposite-based sensor for selective, trace-level ammonia detection at room temperature in the range 0.5-150 ppm, suitable for both environmental monitoring and non-invasive breath analysis for renal disease diagnosis.

title: Ab initio study of precipitate stability in an Mg–Zn–Zr–Y alloy and its effects on corrosion, antimicrobial and in vitro biocompatibility
abstract: DFT study of precipitate stability in an Mg–5Zn–0.5Zr–0.9Y alloy reveals that the Mg 24 Y 5 phase is more stable than the Mg 7 Zn 3 phase. The alloy exhibited excellent antibacterial activity without deteriorating cytocompatibility.

title: Fiber-reinforced zwitterionic elastomer composites for artificial heart valves
abstract: Fiber-reinforced zwitterionic elastomer composites have been developed for application in artificial heart valves, integrating exceptional anticoagulant and antifouling properties with anisotropic mechanical characteristics.

title: CMCS/PEGDA-based photoresponsive hydrogel with high efficiency for gingival retraction and rapid hemostasis
abstract: Injectable CP hydrogel gingival retraction with photocuring, swelling, and excellent hemostasis. (CMCS: carboxymethyl chitosan. PEGDA: polyethylene glycol diacrylate).

title: A shape-adaptive hydrogel with dual antibacterial and osteogenic properties for alveolar bone defect repair
abstract: A shape-adaptive hydrogel with sequential antibacterial and osteogenic functions was proposed to repair irregular osteoporosis bone defects. Naringin was incorporated into microspheres and dispersed into GelMA to repair mandibular defects in rats and alveolar bone defects in osteoporotic rats.

title: A surface modified laser-induced graphene based flexible biosensor for multiplexed sweat analysis
abstract: NiO–Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene-modified flexible laser-induced graphene (LIG) electrode for the separate and concurrent analysis of ascorbic acid (AA), dopamine (DA), and uric acid (UA) in human sweat.

title: Ultrasonic activation of polymer–drug conjugates for targeted and combinational pancreatic cancer therapy
abstract: Well defined polymer–drug conjugates eliciting sonochemically assisted drug release enabling synergistic therapeutic effects against a pancreatic cancer cell line are reported.

title: pH-Independent charge-reversal strategy for enhanced tumor penetration based on hyaluronidase-responsive tellurium-containing polycarbonate nanocarriers
abstract: We present a tumor-penetration strategy involving pH-independent hyaluronidase-responsive cationic tellurium-containing polycarbonate nanoparticles (HPNTPs) that can actively infiltrate throughout the tumor tissue through transcytosis.

title: Phenoxazine-based ambipolar luminescent room-temperature liquid crystals capable of being used in bioimaging applications
abstract: A new class of phenoxazine-based luminescent liquid crystal molecules exhibiting room temperature liquid crystallinity, ambipolar charge carrier mobility which also can be used for bioimaging were synthesized.

title: Versatility of LNPs across different administration routes for targeted RNA delivery
abstract: The advent of lipid nanoparticle (LNP) technology has marked a significant breakthrough in the field of drug delivery, offering unprecedented opportunities in gene therapy, vaccine delivery, and personalized medicine.

title: Enhanced mRNA and pDNA delivery via PEGylated solid lipid nanoparticles with an optimally balanced ionizable/cationic lipid content
abstract: This study presents the development of PEGylated solid lipid nanoparticles with an optimally balanced ionizable/cationic lipid content as an efficient and nontoxic delivery vehicle for diverse nucleic acid cargoes.

title: A Bi 13 S 18 I 2 -based wearable photoelectrochemical biosensor for accurate monitoring of l -tyrosine in sweat as a diabetes biomarker
abstract: The development of real-time, non-invasive, flexible wearable systems for biomarker monitoring is critical for advancing healthcare diagnostics.

title: Improving extracellular matrix penetration with biocatalytic metal–organic framework nanoswimmers
abstract: Catalase and collagenase dual-enzyme functionalized nanoswimmers degrade the extracellular matrix and self-propel through the tumor microenvironment, enhancing penetration and improving intratumoral drug delivery.

title: Multi-responsive shape memory and self-healing hydrogels with gold and silver nanoparticles
abstract: Nanocomposite smart gels (Nc- x ) with self-healing and shape memory properties were designed in different types and size nano particles with temperature or light stimuli.

title: Suppression of the HSP90-HIF1α pathway with SNX2112-encapsulated nano-micelles for effective triple-negative breast cancer photothermal combined photodynamic therapy
abstract: Enhanced photothermal and photodynamic therapy by supressing of the HSP90-HIF1α pathway is a promising strategy for the treatment of triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) as it can accurately target tumor tissues and improve therapeutic efficacy.

title: 3D printed polycaprolactone/phosphoester-modified poly(amino acid)–graphene oxide scaffold for meniscal regeneration
abstract: Phosphoester (PE) units were copolymerized with poly(amino acids) (PAAs), then blended with graphene oxide (GO) and polycaprolactone (PCL) via 3D-printing to fabricate PCL/P–P–GO scaffolds for promoting meniscal regeneration.

title: Nitrogen doped carbon quantum dots: a multifaceted carbon nanomaterial that interferes in an amyloid-forming trajectory
abstract: LAdN-CQDs have been used to inhibit fibril formation, display biocompatibility, reduce reactive oxygen species, and safeguard against apoptosis, indicating promise in neuroprotection.

title: Ultrasound-induced piezoionic hydrogels with antibacterial and antioxidant properties for promoting infected diabetic wound healing
abstract: A new class of ultrasound-induced piezoionic hydrogels with antibacterial and antioxidant properties are developed to accelerate infected diabetic wound healing.

title: Retraction: Hydrogel microneedles with multifunctional strategy for prolonged hyperuricemia management
abstract: Retraction of ‘Hydrogel microneedles with a multifunctional strategy for prolonged hyperuricemia management’ by Rui Wang et al. , J. Mater. Chem. B , 2025, Accepted Manuscript, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4TB02590C.

title: Insulin-producing INS-1 cell cultures on biomimetic 3D scaffolds
abstract: The morphology of grown INS-1 cultures from spheroids to sheets is strongly affected by the properties of the 3D fiber scaffold.

title: Polymeric nanoparticles as a platform for nose-to-brain delivery

abstract: Novel development and characterisation of dual and sequential bioceramic porous drug delivery systems in two morphologies, with the release of antimicrobial agents in the first 2 weeks, followed by bone regenerative agents up to 4 to 5 weeks.

title: Aqueous-stable Mn( ii )-MOF nanoparticles with high r 1 relaxivity and biocompatibility: a novel T 1 MRI contrast agent
abstract: The demand for safe, effective, and multifunctional magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) contrast agents (CAs) continues to drive the search for gadolinium-free alternatives.

title: Drug delivery systems loaded with plant-derived natural products for dental caries prevention and treatment
abstract: Schematic diagram of drug delivery system for dental caries prevention and treatment.

title: Calcium loaded biodegradable porous microspheres for the hemostasis of coagulopathy
abstract: Porous biodegradable microspheres loaded with Ca 2+ are developed for hemostasis of coagulopathy.

title: Bioactive wet adhesive materials for oral health: strategies and applications
abstract: Synopsis of bioactive wet-adhesive materials for oral health: stable adhesion via hydration-layer disruption, interfacial linkages, and cohesion reinforcement; integrated bioactive effects for diverse oral applications.

title: Surface Bi-vacancy and corona polarization engineered nanosheets with sonopiezocatalytic antibacterial activity for wound healing
abstract: BiOBr-BiVP nanosheets enhance piezoelectric properties by improving charge separation and migration. Meanwhile, Bi vacancies increase adsorption and reaction sites for ROS and CO generation, achieving the bacterial eradication and wound healing.

title: Development of 3D-printed conducting microneedle-based electrochemical point-of-care device for transdermal sensing of chlorpromazine
abstract: We have demonstrated a straightforward and scalable manufacturing process for the development of a 3D-printed conducting microneedle array-based electrochemical point-of-care device for the minimally invasive sensing of chlorpromazine.

title: Construction of flexible and stable β-glycine/polyvinyl alcohol nanofibers with unidirectional polarization orientation via electrospinning
abstract: High-performance aligned β-glycine/PVA bio-piezoelectric films with excellent comprehensive properties fabricated via a simple electrospinning method.

title: Microgel-encapsulated tetrandrine nanoparticles promote spinal cord repair by sustaining neuroinflammation inhibition
abstract: By embedding Tet nanoparticles in microgels, we developed a dual-drug system for controlled drug delivery to restrain neuroinflammation in SCI.

title: Morphological insights in oxidative sensitive nanocarrier pharmacokinetics, targeting, and photodynamic therapy
abstract: Nanoparticle (NP) morphology holds significant importance in nanomedicine, particularly concerning its implications for biological responses.

title: Multi-layered electrode constructs for neural tissue engineering
abstract: The study describes the development of a multi-layered electrode coating that was electrochemically characterised. A numerical model of the electric field was developed, and astrocytes were encapsulated within the biosynthetic layer.

title: 3D printable PCL- b -P(MMA- co -TMSPMA)/silica hybrids using a PCL RAFT agent
abstract: Silica/poly(ε-caprolactone-methacrylate) hybrids were produced with PCL- b -P(methyl methacrylate- co -3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl methacrylate) synthesised using PCL-OH as a RAFT agent for controlled polymerization and 3D printed by direct ink writing.

title: Multifunctional Fe 3 O 4 mesocrystals for cancer therapy: integrating hyperthermia and targeted drug delivery
abstract: Comparative study discusses saturation magnetization, cytotoxicity, hyperthermia of Fe 3 O 4 mesocrystals and nanoparticles synthesized via hydrothermal route. Paclitaxel-loaded mesocrystals exhibits enhanced cytotoxicity against HCT-116 cancer cells.

title: Hybrid AuNP@GCD nanosensor for dual-mode dopamine detection and imaging in the Drosophila brain

abstract: Bone defects resulting from trauma or diseases that lead to bone loss have created a growing need for innovative materials suitable for treating bone-related conditions.

abstract: The effect of the flexible linker length in the dimeric prodrugs was explained by their aggregation structures and revealed through molecular dynamics simulation.

title: Structure–reactivity based control of radical-mediated degradation in thiol–Michael hydrogels

abstract: Position, and number, of linkers between aptamer and polymer is critical for the performance of aptamer-molecularly imprinted polymer hybrids.

title: Carbon nanostructures with antibacterial and wound healing activities: recent progress and challenges
abstract: Carbon-based nanostructures acted as potential agents for antibacterial and wound healing properties.

abstract: Innovative potentiometric sensor utilizing electrically conductive copolymer of 2,2′-bithiophene and 1,2-bis( o -aminophenoxy)ethane- N , N , N ′, N ′-tetracarboxylic acid for the selective detection of calcium ions.

abstract: A long-acting acid-resistant gel bead with a core–shell structure was developed, featuring super absorption, high modulus, and sustained floating release, making it a promising gastric retention drug carrier.

abstract: Granular hydrogels, characterized by microporosity, structural heterogeneity, and superior shear-thinning behavior, represent a promising next-generation approach for the treatment of intrauterine adhesions (IUAs) and other intrauterine disorders. abstract: The schematic representation of biomedical applications of short peptides in cancer therapy, regenerative medicine, and tissue engineering.
